title: Invoice PRO-7891 — unexpected token spike during maintenance window
preview:
From: Samir Rao <samir@quickcart.io>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
Cc: QuickCart Finance <billing@quickcart.io>
Date: Fri, 02 Oct 2026 08:12:00 -0700
Subject: Invoice PRO-7891 — apparent token spike during backup window

Ben —

We just received invoice PRO-7891 and there is a large overage line for 2,640,312 tokens on 2026-09-28. Our internal telemetry and sampling show ~1.1M tokens for the same timeframe. The spike appears to coincide with a scheduled backup/ingestion window at 02:00 UTC where we had increased ingestion throughput, but nothing that should generate that magnitude of tokens.

I've attached a CSV export of our request logs (quickcart_token_export_2026-09-28.csv) with request IDs, timestamps, and token counts for your team to reconcile. Can you please: 
- Confirm how Redwood attributes tokens to customers during short spikes and maintenance windows
- Provide the server-side token export you used to populate the invoice
- Pause collections on this invoice while we reconcile (we're close to our payment deadline)

We're happy to hop on a quick call if it's easier — this will block our AP team from approving payment otherwise.

Thanks,
Samir Rao
Head of Platform
QuickCart Inc.
---
Attachments: quickcart_token_export_2026-09-28.csv (CSV, 2.1MB)

From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
To: Samir Rao <samir@quickcart.io>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.ai>
Date: Fri, 02 Oct 2026 09:03:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Invoice PRO-7891 — apparent token spike during backup window

Samir —

Thanks for sending this over and for the CSV — I've received it. I'm going to loop in Billing (Marissa) and our Runtime team (Marcus) to do a side-by-side reconciliation. Preliminary notes:

- We will pull the token attribution export for your account for 2026-09-28 and compare request_id-level counts.
- Please confirm the range you want us to consider (your CSV looks like 01:50:00–03:10:00 UTC). If there are additional off-hours windows, call them out.
- If possible, send 3 representative request IDs from your CSV that you believe are mismatched — that helps our engineers trace the exact request path.

Action plan / timing: 
1) Marcus will pull the server-side export and check for duplicate deliveries / retry storms.
2) Marissa will prepare a provisional billing delta and propose a credit if we identify attribution error.
3) I'll coordinate a 30m sync with your engineering lead if needed — propose today at 2pm PT.

I will also ask Billing to flag the invoice for review so collections are paused until we have the initial findings.

Ben
Ben Carter
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.ai | +1 415-555-0192

From: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>
To: Samir Rao <samir@quickcart.io>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.ai>
Date: Fri, 02 Oct 2026 11:22:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Invoice PRO-7891 — preliminary billing review and next steps

Hi Samir —

Thanks for the export and Ben for the heads up. We've completed a first-pass reconciliation for 2026-09-28 and wanted to share a quick summary and next steps. Attached is a short PDF with the row-level comparison (prelim-billing-breakdown-PRO-7891.pdf).

Summary (preliminary):
- Redwood invoice shows 2,640,312 tokens attributed between 01:45–03:15 UTC.
- Our server-side logs include a cluster of duplicate request deliveries during 02:03–02:11 UTC that were retried by a downstream consumer. Those retried deliveries were, in our opinion, incorrectly double-counted once they retried with a different request_id but identical payload.
- Matching your sample rows indicates ~1.15M unique tokens (matches what you reported) plus ~1.48M from duplicated deliveries, which is how the invoice number was reached.

What we need from you to confirm root cause: 
- A short list (3–5) of request IDs from your CSV that you want us to cross-check; for faster tracing, include the exact timestamp(s).
- Confirmation whether QuickCart treats retries from the streaming consumer as idempotent on your side (i.e., you de-duplicate before consumption).

Proposed commercial remediation (preliminary and subject to final verification):
- If duplicates were caused by our delivery path / routing retries, Redwood will issue a one-time credit for the duplicated token volume. Based on current numbers, we estimate a credit of ~$2,200.
- We can apply the credit to invoice PRO-7891 or issue a proforma credit note, whatever helps your AP team.
- If duplicates were caused upstream on QuickCart (consumer retries without idempotency), we'll document the mismatch and provide the row-level evidence but will not apply a credit.

Next steps: Marcus will paste a couple of matched examples in a follow-up email to aid the engineering investigation. If it helps, we can also share an aggregated token export for the 30‑minute spike window.

I'll put the invoice on hold in our collections system pending your confirmation and the next set of request IDs.

Best,
Marissa Cole
Billing Operations Lead, Redwood Inference
marissa.cole@redwood.ai
---
Attachments: prelim-billing-breakdown-PRO-7891.pdf (PDF, 420KB)
Related: https://app.hubspot.com/contacts/12345/deal/PRO-7891



From: Samir Rao <samir@quickcart.io>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>, QuickCart Finance <billing@quickcart.io>
Date: Fri, 02 Oct 2026 14:05:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Invoice PRO-7891 — preliminary billing review and next steps

Marissa / Ben —

Thanks for the quick turnaround and the explanation. We appreciate Redwood flagging the invoice for review. Per your request, here are 4 request IDs from our export that show the discrepancy (timestamps in UTC):

- rq_20260928_020307_az12 (2026-09-28T02:03:07Z) — tokens: 12,410
- rq_20260928_020309_az15 (2026-09-28T02:03:09Z) — tokens: 9,834
- rq_20260928_020406_az03 (2026-09-28T02:04:06Z) — tokens: 14,220
- rq_20260928_020657_az09 (2026-09-28T02:06:57Z) — tokens: 8,132

We confirm that our consumer layer does de-duplicate by request_id, but we do not dedupe on payload similarity if a new request_id is observed. If duplicates are a result of your delivery retries that manifested as new request IDs, we agree that a credit is appropriate.

Please apply the provisional credit to PRO-7891 while you complete the verification and share the final row-level mapping when available — our AP team will accept a proforma credit note. If you want to jump on the 2pm PT sync Ben suggested, Samir (me) and our SRE will be available.

Samir
Head of Platform, QuickCart
---
Quoted text follows.

title: SOC2/SIG: FinOptima VM evidence transfer & witness session
preview:
From: Allyson Pierce <allyson.pierce@finoptima.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-10-01T09:12:00-07:00
Subject: Request: SOC 2 SIG/VM evidence + live witness session

Hi Ben,

We're preparing for our upcoming SOC 2 readiness review and FinOptima's auditors have a handful of SIG questions focused on vulnerability management. Can you help with the following?

- Full scan cadence and tooling (authenticated vs unauthenticated, agent vs network).
- CVE triage and remediation SLAs by severity (what you commit to, and how you measure).
- Proof artifacts we can hand to auditors (scan run IDs, signed closure letters, patch manifests, ticket links).
- Whether Redwood is able to do a short live witness (30-45 minutes) where a security engineer walks through the scan platform and artifacts.

I've attached our current question list: FinOptima_SIG_Questions_v2.xlsx. Auditor timeline is tight - they'd like evidence handed off by Oct 12 and would prefer the witness session early next week if possible.

Can you confirm what you can provide and a couple of available time slots for the live session?

Thanks,
Allyson Pierce
Security Compliance Lead
FinOptima

Attachments: FinOptima_SIG_Questions_v2.xlsx (xlsx)

From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Allyson Pierce <allyson.pierce@finoptima.com>
Cc: Kimberly Park <kimberly_park@redwood.com>, Monica Patel <monica_patel@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-10-01T11:30:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Request: SOC 2 SIG/VM evidence + live witness session

Hi Allyson - thanks for the note and the attachment. Looping in Kimberly (security) and Monica (CS) to assemble the evidence and run the witness.

Proposed plan:
1) Kimberly will prepare a consolidated evidence packet (scan cadence, SLA table, sample signed closure letters, and an index CSV) and attach a short manifest.
2) We'll schedule a 45-minute witness session for a walkthrough + artifact handoff. Options: Oct 8 (AM PT) or Oct 9 (late AM/early PM PT).
3) We'll provide a DocuSign attestation for the CVE remediation letter if your auditors require a signed statement.

I attached a starter index (vm_evidence_index.csv) so you can see the format we'll use for artifacts.

Let us know which of the proposed slots works, or suggest two alternatives.

Cheers,
Ben Carter
Enterprise Sales
Redwood Inference

Attachments: vm_evidence_index.csv (csv)

From: Kimberly Park <kimberly_park@redwood.com>
To: Allyson Pierce <allyson.pierce@finoptima.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Cc: Monica Patel <monica_patel@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-10-02T08:05:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Request: SOC 2 SIG/VM evidence + live witness session

Allyson / Ben - thanks, I can own the technical deliverables. Below is a concise summary of what Redwood will provide and the operational SLAs we commit to for evidence and CVE handling. I included a proposed witness agenda and the artifact list we intend to hand over.

-- Scan posture and cadence
- Authenticated network/host scans: weekly (full authenticated run against prod/networks under scoped VPC).
- Agent-based telemetry (endpoints & containers): continuous; daily aggregated find list exported for evidence.
- External/Internet-facing scanning: nightly, with recheck on failure.
- Formal third-party penetration test: quarterly (or upon major release), with executive summary provided to customers.

-- CVE triage & remediation SLAs (operational commitments)
- P0 / Critical (active exploit or root cause impacting confidentiality/integrity): initial response within 4 hours, patch/fix or mitigation within 24 hours when feasible (hotfix cadence).
- P1 / High: initial triage within 24 hours, remediation or compensating control in place within 72 hours where possible; otherwise documented exception & timeline.
- P2 / Medium: remediation planned and scheduled within 14 calendar days.
- P3 / Low: remediation in regular maintenance cycle, target within 90 days.

We track these with our internal ticketing + patch manifest. The evidence bundle will include:
- scan_policies.md (scope and config used)
- vm_evidence_manifest.pdf (signed index of artifacts)
- individual scan exports (CSV/JSON) with run IDs and timestamps
- sample closure letters per CVE (signed by our Sec Ops lead)
- patch manifests with package hashes and deployment job ids
- links to issue tracker tickets and CI runs

-- Witness session (proposed agenda, 45 minutes)
1) 00:00-00:20: demo of scan platform and configuration (authenticated run + sample findings)
2) 00:20-00:35: walk through a recent P1 -> closure example (ticket -> patch -> validation)
3) 00:35-00:45: artifact handoff & Q&A (how to verify run IDs, signed closure letters)

Attachments I will provide: vm_evidence_manifest.pdf, scan_policies.md.

If Oct 8 or Oct 9 works for your auditors, please confirm a preferred window (I recommend Oct 8 10:00-10:45 PT). Also confirm if the auditors need a DocuSign-signed attestation or if a signed PDF is sufficient - we can do either.

On Thu, Oct 1, 2026 at 11:30 AM Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com> wrote:
> Proposed plan:
> 1) Kimberly will prepare a consolidated evidence packet...
> 2) We'll schedule a 45-minute witness session...

Thanks,
Kimberly Park
Head of Security Operations
Redwood Inference

Attachments: vm_evidence_manifest.pdf (pdf), scan_policies.md (text)

From: Allyson Pierce <allyson.pierce@finoptima.com>
To: Kimberly Park <kimberly_park@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Cc: Sanjay Verma <sanjay.verma@finoptima.com>
Date: 2026-10-02T09:40:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Request: SOC 2 SIG/VM evidence + live witness session

Hi Kimberly - this is perfect, thanks.

We can do Oct 8 at 10:00 PT for the witness session. Please send a calendar invite (with a link to the Drive evidence folder) and a DocuSign link for the attestation if you can; our audit team prefers an e-signed statement but will accept a signed PDF if necessary.

Quick confirmation items for the evidence packet:
- Please include a sample P1 closure letter and the ticket link for the sample you show during the demo.
- Include run IDs and timestamps for the scans you surface in the witness so the auditors can cross-check.
- If possible, provide a contact for follow-up questions (phone or direct Slack).

If you send the drive folder link and DocuSign by EOD Oct 5 that will keep us on the audit timeline. Appreciate the quick turnaround.

Best,
Allyson Pierce
Security Compliance Lead
FinOptima

References: HubSpot ticket FT-9876

title: Post-call: Reshaping POC roadmap & updated success criteria
preview:
From: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
To: Elena Soto <elena@shoploom.com>
Cc: Monica Patel <monica@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 1 Oct 2026 15:12:00 -0700
Subject: Post-call recap + proposed trial plan (next steps)

Hi Elena — great to meet today, thanks for walking through Shoploom's cart-abandonment and product-suggestion use cases. Quick recap and proposed next steps so we have a single source of truth:  

Summary of what we heard from Shoploom:
- Primary objective: improve add-to-cart conversion by surfacing personalized product suggestions in cart and at checkout.
- Secondary objective: reduce false positives in intent routing (reduce manual reviews).
- Data notes: 6 months of anonymized cart and session logs available in S3; PII lives in a separate store and will not be ingested.

Proposed POC scope (v1):
- Ingest 3 weeks of session+cart logs for training/eval (Karthik owner)
- Model endpoints for suggestion generation + reranking (Redwood hosted)
- Eval: suggestion relevance (precision@5) + latency p95 target initially <= 900ms (Monica owner for metrics)
- Success criteria: +6% add-to-cart lift in A/B sandbox OR precision@5 >= 0.45 with acceptable latency and no data leakage.

Attachments: I attached POC-Plan-v1.docx and a working SuccessCriteria spreadsheet (POC-SuccessCriteria-v2.xlsx).

Requests / next steps:
1) Can you confirm Shoploom is comfortable with the proposed hosts and that your security team will accept Redwood's standard SOC2 + VPC peering checklist? (If not, please flag which items.)
2) Confirm owners on your side for data export and validation (we expect one data engineer + one PM).
3) If the scope looks OK we'll schedule a 60-min kickoff next week to lock timelines (I propose Oct 6 for a kickoff, but flexible).

If you want to expand the scope during the trial we can rebaseline; best practice is to lock a minimal acceptance set and identify "stretch" use cases so we can measure incrementally.

Thanks — I'll hold the kickoff slot tentatively for Oct 6.

Best,
Ben Carter
Senior Solutions Engineer
Redwood Inference
---
Attachments referenced: POC-Plan-v1.docx, POC-SuccessCriteria-v2.xlsx

From: Elena Soto <elena@shoploom.com>

From: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>

Cc: Monica Patel <monica@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 1 Oct 2026 15:12:00 -0700
Subject: Post-call recap + proposed trial plan (next steps)

Hi Elena — great to meet today, thanks for walking through Shoploom's cart-abandonment and product-suggestion use cases. Quick recap and proposed next steps so we have a single source of truth:  

Summary of what we heard from Shoploom:
- Primary objective: improve add-to-cart conversion by surfacing personalized product suggestions in cart and at checkout.
- Secondary objective: reduce false positives in intent routing (reduce manual reviews).
- Data notes: 6 months of anonymized cart and session logs available in S3; PII lives in a separate store and will not be ingested.

Proposed POC scope (v1):
- Ingest 3 weeks of session+cart logs for training/eval (Karthik owner)
- Model endpoints for suggestion generation + reranking (Redwood hosted)
- Eval: suggestion relevance (precision@5) + latency p95 target initially <= 900ms (Monica owner for metrics)
- Success criteria: +6% add-to-cart lift in A/B sandbox OR precision@5 >= 0.45 with acceptable latency and no data leakage.

Attachments: I attached POC-Plan-v1.docx and a working SuccessCriteria spreadsheet (POC-SuccessCriteria-v2.xlsx).

Requests / next steps:
1) Can you confirm Shoploom is comfortable with the proposed hosts and that your security team will accept Redwood's standard SOC2 + VPC peering checklist? (If not, please flag which items.)
2) Confirm owners on your side for data export and validation (we expect one data engineer + one PM).
3) If the scope looks OK we'll schedule a 60-min kickoff next week to lock timelines (I propose Oct 6 for a kickoff, but flexible).

If you want to expand the scope during the trial we can rebaseline; best practice is to lock a minimal acceptance set and identify "stretch" use cases so we can measure incrementally.

Thanks — I'll hold the kickoff slot tentatively for Oct 6.

Best,
Ben Carter
Senior Solutions Engineer
Redwood Inference

title: Urgent: Production impact — decode stalls & latency spike after v5.1 rollout
Demo invite — time showing incorrectly after forward
Mutual action plan — acceptance sentinels, exit gates, and sign-off path
preview:
From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
To: Evan Brooks <evan.b@mediapp.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>
Date: 2026-09-28T16:12:00Z
Subject: Draft MAP + acceptance sentinels for pilot (first pass)

Evan — great talking earlier. As promised, I sketched a first-pass mutual action plan focused on how MediApp and Redwood will evaluate the pilot and what counts as a pass/fail. High level goals: reduce per-request latency for the clinical summarization call to under 250ms p50 while keeping accuracy within 1% of your current baseline, and validate data isolation + auditing for PHI workflows.

Attached: MAP-v1.xlsx (tabbed: milestones, acceptance-criteria, test-cases).

Key asks from us to you (to lock down acceptance tests):
- Provide 3 representative production prompt sets (anonymized) by Oct 6
- Confirm targeted traffic profile (qps and sequence lengths) for smoke run
- Identify the primary integration owner and procurement contact for contract steps

Proposed acceptance sentinels (top-level):
1) Latency sentinel: p50 <= 250ms, p95 <= 600ms over 24-hour smoke run
2) Accuracy sentinel: automated concordance test >= 99% for structured fields (name, dob, medcodes) and human review concordance within ±1% for free-text summaries (sample n=100)
3) Reliability sentinel: 99.5% success rate (no server errors) during test window
4) Security sentinel: SOC2 snapshot + audit logs visible to MediApp in our console; KMS integration verified

If these look reasonable I’ll circulate a version we can attach to the SOW and route through procurement. Happy to hop on a short call to align next steps.

— Ben
Senior Solutions 
Redwood Inference

Attachment stub: MAP-v1.xlsx (spreadsheet)


From: Evan Brooks <evan.b@mediapp.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
Cc: procurement@mediapp.com
Date: 2026-09-29T10:03:00Z
Subject: Re: Draft MAP + acceptance sentinels for pilot (first pass)

Thanks Ben — appreciate the crisp list. A few clarifying points before we lock the MAP: 

- The latency targets look aggressive but plausible. Our current on-prem baseline for the same call is p50 ~ 300ms, p95 ~ 700ms. We can try to meet p50 250ms but we need to know what hardware profile you'll run the pilot on (GPU type/burst policy) so we can compare apples-to-apples.
- For accuracy / human review: can we define the sampling methodology? We prefer stratified sampling across three clinician cohorts.
- Security: procurement needs the SOC2 report + an expected data retention policy and audit window (we retain logs for 7 years).

I’m looping procurement for the formal SOW path. Our technical lead might ping Karthik with a few questions about kv-cache and tokenization differences.

— Evan
Director of Product, MediApp

On Wed, Sep 28, 2026 at 4:12 PM Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai> wrote:
> Evan — great talking earlier. As promised, I sketched a first-pass mutual action plan...


From: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>
To: Evan Brooks <evan.b@mediapp.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
Cc: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.ai>
Date: 2026-09-30T14:45:00Z
Subject: Re: Draft MAP + acceptance sentinels for pilot (first pass) — test scope and sign-off path

Evan — good points. I updated the MAP attachment with a proposed sampling plan and a sign-off pathway that keeps acceptance criteria objective. Summary of the updates: 

1) Sampling methodology (tab: sample-plan): stratified by clinician cohort (ED, primary care, specialist), random n=120 (40 per cohort) for human review. That should give ~±1% margin.

2) Hardware profile (tab: infra): pilot on g5x2-equivalent instance with reserved capacity for the 48-hour smoke run. We’ll provide a short spec sheet so you can compare with your on-prem baseline.

3) Pass/fail matrix (tab: acceptance):
- Green: All sentinels met for two consecutive 24-hour windows → automatic progression to cutover prep
- Yellow: One sentinel out of threshold → investigate, mitigation sprint (3 business days) then retest
- Red: More than one sentinel failing or security gaps → hold and escalate to execs for decision

4) Escalation path: named execs for quick decision: Evan (MediApp), Ben (Redwood), Marissa (Redwood) — and procurement contacts on both sides for contract sign-off.

Action items I can own this week: finalize sign-off table for inclusion in SOW and send to procurement. Can you confirm the clinician cohorts and procurement contact?

— Marissa
Account Executive, Redwood Inference

Attachment stub: MAP-v1.xlsx (updated: sample-plan, infra, acceptance tabs)


From: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.ai>
To: Evan Brooks <evan.b@mediapp.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
Date: 2026-10-01T09:16:00Z
Subject: Re: Draft MAP — technical smoke tests and acceptance automation

Evan — quick note from the infra side on runnable smoke tests and what we will measure automatically: 

Automated smoke-run checklist (to be executed during two 24-hour windows):
- Synthetic workload (scripted qps) aligned to your supplied traffic profile (we need the seq length distribution)
- P50/P95 token latency measurements (client-observed and server-side)
- Error taxonomy: 5xx/timeout/validation errors logged separately
- KV cache hit-rate metric: target >= 65% during sustained load (we’ll provide caching config)
- Memory/host metrics: GPU utilization, OOM events, scheduler backpressure

Acceptance automation: we’ll push the raw logs to a secure share and run the concordance harness. For the human-review step we’ll provide an overlap set of 25 records that both teams review to validate grader alignment.

One constraint: if procurement requires 7-year log retention inside your environment, we need to scope for a private deployment (Private) rather than Hosted — that will change cost and timeline. I flag this early so procurement has the right SOW options.

I’ll add the smoke-run scripts to the Drive folder and tag the tests in the MAP spreadsheet.

— Karthik
Principal Solutions Engineer, Redwood Inference

Attachment stub: SOC2-snapshot-2026-09.pdf (security summary)


From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
To: Evan Brooks <evan.b@mediapp.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.ai>
Cc: procurement@mediapp.com
Date: 2026-10-02T09:18:00Z
Subject: Re: Draft MAP + acceptance sentinels — sign-off pathway and next steps

Thanks everyone — this is shaping up. Quick summary and next steps so procurement has a single path to follow: 

1) Ben will attach the finalized MAP and push an SOW addendum that includes the pass/fail matrix and the 48-hour smoke-run windows.
2) MediApp provides: three anonymized prompt sets, clinician cohort confirmation, and procurement contact by Oct 6.
3) Redwood provides: infra spec, smoke-run script, SOC2 snapshot (attached), and a DocuSign-ready SOW addendum for signature.

Decision ladder for cutover:
- If both 24-hour windows green → agreed cutover runway (2 weeks buffer for config + rollout)
- If any one window yellow → 3-business-day mitigation then retest; execs to approve (Evan/Ben/Marissa)
- If red → pause deployment and open executive review (schedule within 48 hours)

Procurement — please expect the SOW addendum and DocuSign link from us today. Evan — if the 7-year retention requirement remains, call that out and we’ll switch to Private deployment options and update pricing; otherwise hosted reserved capacity profile is the default.

Thanks — I’ll send the DocuSign and final MAP link in a follow-up.

— Ben
Senior Solutions
Redwood Inference

title: Executive sponsor routing + EB window orchestration for Q4 close
preview:
From: Alex Martinez <alex.martinez@redwood.com>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>
Cc: Sarah Patel <sarah.patel@acme-corp.com>
Date: 2026-09-26T09:12:00-07:00
Subject: Executive sponsor routing + EB window orchestration for Q4 close

Hi team,

Following last week's demo and the draft MAP I attached (MAP_acme_v2.xlsx), Acme is within reach but we need a short executive push to lock contract language and finalize deployment timelines. Two asks:

1) Sponsor routing: I sketched a primary/backup sponsor map on tab \"Exec Map\" — can you both review and confirm owners? Suggested primary is Sarah Patel (CIO) and backup is Tom Rivera (SVP Product). If Priya (CS) needs to be looped as sponsor steward, flag it.

2) EB (executive briefing) windows: Acme asked for a 60-minute EB with execs + procurement for final alignment. Proposed windows (all PT): Oct 6 AM (9–10), Oct 7 PM (2–3), Oct 12 AM (8–9). Please mark availability or propose alternatives.

I've included a short evaluation timeline on tab \"EvalPlan\" — we need owners for the final technical eval (app perf checklist) and the compliance questionnaire.

Attachments: MAP_acme_v2.xlsx (skeleton MAP & roles), EB_candidate_list.pdf (exec attendee suggestions).

Next step: once we confirm sponsor owners and an EB window I'll draft the invite and a 1-page agenda for Sarah.

Thanks,
Alex
Alex Martinez
Account Executive | Redwood Inference
(alex.martinez@redwood.com)

---


From: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
To: Alex Martinez <alex.martinez@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>
Cc: Sarah Patel <sarah.patel@acme-corp.com>
Date: 2026-09-26T11:03:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Executive sponsor routing + EB window orchestration for Q4 close

Alex — thanks for pulling this together. Quick notes after reviewing MAP_acme_v2:

- Sponsor map: good start. I agree Sarah primary, Tom backup. Recommend we designate Priya Natarajan as \"Sponsor Steward\" to handle logistics and follow-ups; she can own the MAP checklist items 1.2 (security evidence) and 2.1 (PO execution).
- EB windows: Oct 6 AM works best for the execs I run with (internal calendar permitting). Oct 12 AM is tight for several leaders.
- Evaluation owners: I can take the compliance questionnaire and coordinate with Security for the SOC2 artifacts. Karthik, can you take the performance/demo checklist?

If we lock Oct 6 9–10 PT, I suggest the agenda: 10min intros, 20min technical highlights (latency / cost controls), 15min procurement/Q&A, 15min next steps & signatures path.

Marissa
Marissa Cole
VP, Head of Sales | Redwood Inference

---


From: Sarah Patel <sarah.patel@acme-corp.com>
To: Alex Martinez <alex.martinez@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
Cc: Tom Rivera <tom.rivera@acme-corp.com>
Date: 2026-09-28T08:21:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Executive sponsor routing + EB window orchestration for Q4 close

Thanks Alex and Marissa — appreciate the clear plan. Confirming: Sarah Patel (CIO) will be primary sponsor, Tom (SVP Product) is backup. Adding Procurement to the thread; procurement@acme-corp.com should handle contract routing.

Oct 6 9–10 PT looks doable for the execs; can you send a formal invite with an agenda and a short doc that summarizes final acceptance criteria (one pager)? Also please include the compliance artifacts you mentioned so procurement can pre-scan.

Tom has forwarded a note to procurement and looped Tom R and me on timelines.

Sarah
Sarah Patel
CIO | Acme Corporation

---


From: Alex Martinez <alex.martinez@redwood.com>
To: Sarah Patel <sarah.patel@acme-corp.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>
Cc: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.com>, Acme Procurement <procurement@acme-corp.com>
Date: 2026-09-29T10:47:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Executive sponsor routing + EB window orchestration for Q4 close

Great — locking Oct 6 09:00–10:00 PT tentatively. I will:

- Send calendar invite (will include Doc: \"ACME_FinalAcceptance_1pager.pdf\" and attachments referenced earlier).
- Draft one-pager acceptance criteria & a compressed MAP view (will attach MAP_acme_v2 as PDF).
- Assign owners: Karthik = tech demo + perf checklist; Marissa = procurement & pricing concessions; Priya = compliance artifacts & SOC2 packet.

Action items for this thread:
- Karthik: confirm you can lead the demo and whether you want a 30min tech prep with Acme engineers (propose Oct 2).
- Priya: confirm you can own compliance artifacts and whether you need any help pulling logs/audits.
- Procurement@acme-corp.com: if you have any redline deadlines or preferred legal combo (NDA / PO cadence), flag them now so we can align the EB agenda.

I'll circulate the invite by EOD tomorrow.

Thanks — Alex

---


From: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>

Alex / team — I can own the technical demo and the perf checklist. Confirming availability for a 30-min tech prep with Acme on Oct 2 at 11:00 PT. Prep notes I'll cover: model latency at 2k tokens, batching recommendations, cost-by-route tradeoffs, and a quick run-through of KV cache behavior.

Priya — I may need the SOC2 artifact index and a short list of retention policies to reference in the demo appendix. Marissa — please flag any price concession items we should be ready to discuss live.

Once Alex sends the invite, I'll start a short internal prep doc and share a link.

Karthik

Solutions Architect | Redwood Inference

title: DocuVerify: POA & signer routing for Acme MSA/Order Form
preview:
From: Elena Ross <elena.ross@acme.com>
To: Alex Martinez <alex@redwood.com>, Naomi Feldman <naomi@redwood.com>
Cc: Samir Patel <procurement@acme.com>
Date: Mon, 27 Sep 2026 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Re: MSA + Order Form — ready to sign

Hi Alex / Naomi,

Thanks — Acme is ready to proceed with signature on the MSA and Order Form.
We have a temporary POA on file for Samir Patel (VP Finance) so he will execute on behalf of the company for this transaction. I can attach the declaration and a scanned letter from the corporate secretary.

Requested routing: DocuSign to Samir (procurement@acme.com) as primary signer, then to Elena for acknowledgement (elena.ross@acme.com). Please include the Order Form (OrderForm_12345.pdf) as an attachment and the clean MSA we received back from your team.

Do you require any additional evidence of authority? Happy to provide whatever you need.

Best,
Elena Ross
Senior Counsel, Acme Corporation
elena.ross@acme.com

---

> To: Elena Ross <elena.ross@acme.com>
> Cc: Naomi Feldman <naomi@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.com>, Jordan Blake <jordan@redwood.com>
> Date: Mon, Sep 27, 2026 at 9:25 AM
> Subject: Re: MSA + Order Form — ready to sign
>
> Thanks, Elena — good to hear.
>
> Quick asks from our side before we route:
> 1) Please attach the POA declaration and the corporate secretary letter (PDF). We need a signed doc that explicitly authorizes Samir to sign commercial contracts of this size.
> 2) Confirm the exact email address you want DocuSign routed to (procurement@acme.com as in your note?).
> 3) Tell us whether Acme requires any witness or countersignature fields in DocuSign (some customers need a witness line for POA signings).
>
> Karthik / Jordan — tagging you to review the POA formatting once Elena sends it. If legal OKs, Jordan can create the envelope and include the audit-acceptance checkbox and the "signing on behalf of" declarative text.
>
> We plan to send a single envelope for MSA + Order Form; Redwood will sign last after we receive countersigned copies.
>
> Alex Martinez
> Sr. Account Lead, Redwood Inference
> alex@redwood.com | +1 415-555-0123
>
>
> On Mon, 27 Sep 2026 at 09:40 AM Elena Ross <elena.ross@acme.com> wrote:
> > Alex — attaching the POA (Acme_POA_declaration.pdf) and the sec-letter.
> > Samir's primary routing email is procurement@acme.com. There is no external witness required; the corporate secretary letter includes a notarization stamp.
> >
> > I see that Redwood wants Samir to sign and then Redwood to countersign. That works. We prefer the envelope to show the signer name as: "Samir Patel (on behalf of Acme Corporation)" and we will ensure the signer's identity is verified on our end.
> >
> > Attaching: Acme_POA_declaration.pdf, SecLetter_Acme_signed.pdf
> >
> > Elena Ross
> > Senior Counsel, Acme Corporation
> > elena.ross@acme.com

From: Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.com>
To: Alex Martinez <alex@redwood.com>, Jordan Blake <jordan@redwood.com>, Naomi Feldman <naomi@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 27 Sep 2026 12:05:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: POA docs — internal review

All — I reviewed the POA declaration Elena sent. High level: the POA contains the delegation language we need and is notarized. A couple of notes for the envelope: 
- Add a short declarative checkbox under Samir's signature: "I am signing on behalf of Acme Corporation under delegated authority."
- Enable the DocuSign audit trail and retain for 7 years per our retention policy (please set storage flag).
- Add an optional signature field for Elena if Acme wants counsel to acknowledge (not required).

If Jordan can build the envelope with those items, legal is good to proceed. I've attached a quick contract-acceptance checklist (legal_checklist_POA.txt).

Karthik Iyer
Head of Legal Ops, Redwood Inference
karthik@redwood.com

---

To: Elena Ross <elena.ross@acme.com>, Samir Patel <procurement@acme.com>
Cc: Alex Martinez <alex@redwood.com>, Jordan Blake <jordan@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 28 Sep 2026 10:02:00 -07:00
Subject: DocuSign routing and final clean copy

Hi Elena / Samir —

Thanks for the quick turnaround. Per Karthik's notes, we'll include the POA checkbox and enable the audit trail.

Routing plan we will send now:
- Step 1: Samir Patel (procurement@acme.com) — Sign MSA + Order Form; checkbox: "Signing on behalf of Acme Corporation under delegated authority"; attach POA.
- Step 2: Elena Ross (elena.ross@acme.com) — Optional acknowledgement (no required signature).
- Step 3: Alex Martinez (alex@redwood.com) — Redwood review (no signature field; just to receive a copy).
- Step 4: Jordan Blake (jordan@redwood.com) — Final countersignature by Redwood (signature required).

Please confirm you are OK with Samir as primary signer and the routing above. We will include: Acme_MSA_FINAL_clean.pdf and OrderForm_12345.pdf in the envelope.

Target: send envelope today; ask for signature within 48 hours.

Thanks,
Naomi Feldman
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
naomi@redwood.com | +1 415-555-0199

title: Offline depot acceptance: manifest & signer verification
preview:
From: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.com>
To: Jordan Mills <jordan.mills@acme-manufacturing.com>
Cc: Karthik Iyer <karthik_iyer@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 30 Sep 2026 09:03:00 -0700
Subject: Offline depot acceptance: manifest & signer verification

Jordan —

Thanks again for agreeing to the offline depot flow for the pilot install. Attached is the signed delivery manifest bundle we will hand to the courier at Redwood's outbound staging. Quick summary and asks so we can be aligned for acceptance at your depot: 

- What we're handing over: signed_manifest_v3.tar.gz (contains images, checksums, install manifest) + detached signature manifest_v3.sig.
- Acceptance window: Tue Oct 6 — Fri Oct 9, local depot hours 08:00–16:00.
- On-site signers: Acme will need two named signers at receipt (operator + site security). We will supply the manifest and signature files; Acme must verify signatures against the signer roster we exchanged yesterday.
- Proof of custody: courier will present a tamper-sealed crate plus a printed waybill. We will also provide a sideband signed summary (email + sha256) to your distro alias.

Can you confirm: (1) names/emails of the two on-site signers we should list on the manifest; (2) OK for the Oct 6–9 window?

Attachments: signed_manifest_v3.tar.gz (signature: manifest_v3.sig)

Thanks,
Ben Carter
Senior Solutions Engineer
Redwood Inference
ben_carter@redwood.com
+1 415-555-0172


From: Jordan Mills <jordan.mills@acme-manufacturing.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.com>
Cc: Karthik Iyer <karthik_iyer@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 30 Sep 2026 10:18:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Offline depot acceptance: manifest & signer verification

Ben —

Thanks for the summary. A couple of quick clarifications before I confirm the window: 

1) We prefer to list: Dana Rios (dana.rios@acme-manufacturing.com) — Site Ops Lead; and Miguel Alvarez (miguel.alvarez@acme-manufacturing.com) — Facility Security. Are those acceptable?
2) What level of signing verification do you expect on-site? Our standard practice is: check detached sig matches manifest, confirm checksum of the tar, and take photo of crate seal. Do you require an HSM-signed timestamp or anything beyond that?
3) Who should we scan/send the acceptance receipt to after signing?

If everything else is standard, Oct 6–9 works; we just need the final manifest with the two signers populated.

—Jordan
Jordan Mills
Senior IT Manager, Edge Operations
Acme Manufacturing
jordan.mills@acme-manufacturing.com


From: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.com>
To: Jordan Mills <jordan.mills@acme-manufacturing.com>
Cc: Karthik Iyer <karthik_iyer@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.com>, Laura Bennett <laura_bennett@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 30 Sep 2026 11:05:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Offline depot acceptance: manifest & signer verification

Jordan —

Dana and Miguel are perfect, thank you. Answers inline: 

1) I'll update the manifest to include Dana Rios and Miguel Alvarez in the signature block and reattach the tar + detached sig. Expect the updated bundle in <24h>.
2) Standard verification you listed is fine and is what our courier will follow: detached sig verification, checksum verification of the tarball, and a photo of the seal recorded in the courier log. For this pilot we do not require an HSM timestamp on your side — we will provide an auditor-friendly signature chain (manifest_v3.sig) that you can verify offline. If you need a secondary timestamping step we can add it for later releases.
3) Please scan and send the signed acceptance receipt to ops-accept@acme-manufacturing.com and cc: shipping-ops@redwood.com. We will ingest that into the acceptance ledger.

Attachments included in this message: signed_manifest_v3.tar.gz, manifest_v3.sig.

Logistics note for our side: SecureCourier will pick up at Redwood staging on Oct 5 (evening) and will do a morning delivery Oct 6. I've looped in the courier ops alias below to confirm pickup and provide waybill once generated.

Ben


From: Karthik Iyer <karthik_iyer@redwood.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.com>, Jordan Mills <jordan.mills@acme-manufacturing.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.com>, Laura Bennett <laura_bennett@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 30 Sep 2026 12:06:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Offline depot acceptance: manifest & signer verification

Jordan / Ben —

A couple of operational caveats from the infra side to make acceptance frictionless: 

- The manifest bundle includes per-image sha256 checksums in images/checksums.txt. On-site verification should fail fast if any checksum mismatch is detected; please don't attempt to re-sign altered artifacts.
- Key material: the detached signature (manifest_v3.sig) was generated by Redwood's deployment signing key; the public verification key is in the manifest root (pub/deploy_key_v2.pem). If your team wants us to pre-install a copy into your verification appliance, we can deliver pub/deploy_key_v2.pem on a separate signed thumbprint email.
- KMS note: if Acme wants the acceptance event to also register a KMS-wrapped acceptance token, we can coordinate a short key wrap handshake during acceptance (adds ~5–10 minutes at receipt). Let us know if you want that; otherwise we'll proceed with offline signature verification only.

I'll also attach a short checklist as a stub (courier_waybill_998172.pdf) for the receiving team.

-Karthik
Karthik Iyer
Head of Private Deployments
Redwood Inference


From: SecureCourier Ops <ops@securecourier.co>
To: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.com>, Jordan Mills <jordan.mills@acme-manufacturing.com>
Date: Wed, 30 Sep 2026 13:22:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Offline depot acceptance: manifest & signer verification

Ben / Jordan —

Pickup confirmed for Redwood staging on Oct 5, manifest attached to courier record. Waybill attached: courier_waybill_998172.pdf. Tracking ID: SC-998172-ACME. 

On receipt our driver will: verify crate seal, confirm names of signers against manifest, and capture a timestamped photo. If Acme requests KMS-wrapped acceptance we will wait on-site and perform the handshake (please advise if you want the driver to expect an extra 10–12 minutes).

We will email the signed acceptance scan to ops-accept@acme-manufacturing.com and cc shipping-ops@redwood.com immediately after signing.

Regards,
SecureCourier Ops

title: DPA redlines + subprocessor notification & objection logistics
preview:
From: Jordan Smith <jordan.smith@acme-cloud.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>, Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Acme Cloud Legal <legal@acme-cloud.com>
Date: Tue, 28 Sep 2026 15:22:00 -0700
Subject: DPA redlines + subprocessor list and notification window

Ben / Neha,

Attached is our procurement team's redlined DPA for the upcoming integration. Two quick asks up front:

- Can Redwood confirm the subprocessor notification window you offer to customers when you onboard new subprocessors? Our procurement requires at least 60 days so customers can object and/or seek controls.
- We marked a few clauses on objection handling (see §7.3) — can your legal ops confirm the process end-to-end? Specifically: timeline, how objections are surfaced to customers, and fallback remedies.

We also included a current subprocessor inventory (Subprocessor_List_20260928.xlsx). Our legal would like to align on whether Redwood would allow a shorter window (45 days) with an escalated review for high-risk categories.

Attachments:
- Redlined_DPA_Acme_v1.pdf (pdf)
- Subprocessor_List_20260928.xlsx (xlsx)

Can we get a response by end of week? We have an internal board meeting next Wednesday and would like to include the proposed timeline.

Thanks,
Jordan Smith
Sr. Procurement Manager
Acme Cloud Inc.
procurement@acme-cloud.com

---


From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>
To: Jordan Smith <jordan.smith@acme-cloud.com>
Cc: Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwoodinference.com>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwoodinference.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik_iyer@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, 28 Sep 2026 18:03:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: DPA redlines + subprocessor list and notification window

Jordan — thanks for the redline and the list. Quick notes from my side:

- Redwood's standard public customer notice window for new subprocessors is 45 days. For enterprise customers we can extend to 60 days on a case-by-case basis tied to contract language.
- Objection process today: customer submits objection -> we evaluate classification (high/medium/low risk) -> if high, we pause onboarding and engage remediation or mitigation; if unresolved, we facilitate vendor removal or other technical/workflow mitigations. Timeline target is 30 days for initial triage, 60 days to resolution for high-risk objections.

title: DPA: No-learning commitment + EU routing details
preview:
From: Emma Gray <emma.gray@crescenthealth.io>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
Cc: Crescent Health Legal <legal@crescenthealth.io>
Date: Tue, 29 Sep 2026 13:15:00 +0100
Subject: DPA questions — no-learning commitment and EU residency

Hi Ben,

Thanks for the demo last week — Crescent Health is excited about Redwood's latency and ops features. Before we proceed to procurement, legal has two core asks on the DPA we wanted clarity on:

1) No-learning / model improvement: We need an explicit, signed commitment that data submitted via the API will not be used to train or improve models (no adaptive training). Can Redwood provide a standard clause and operational proof steps?

2) EU routing / residency: For patient-identifiable data we require processing within the EU and a list of all subprocessors plus their residency commitments. We also need the KMS residency options documented.

Can you share a redline or proposed DPA amendment and any operational checklists (evidence we can rely on during our security review)?

Appreciate a quick turnaround — our counsel is on a compressed timeline.

Best,
Emma
Emma Gray
Head of Procurement
Crescent Health
---
[Note: attached our standard DPA excerpt for visibility — not required to be returned]

From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
To: Emma Gray <emma.gray@crescenthealth.io>
Date: Tue, 29 Sep 2026 14:08:00 +0100
Subject: Re: DPA questions — no-learning commitment and EU residency

Hi Emma — thanks, glad the demo was useful.

Short answers up front:
- No-learning: yes, we can commit to a contractual no-learning clause for your account. We have an established operational evidence flow (ops attestations + subprocessor attest) we can include as an appendix.
- EU routing: we can route requests to our EU region and provide a subprocessor inventory and KMS residency options (onshore key management via customer-managed KMS is available for Dedicated/Private plans).

I'm looping in Olga (Legal) and Kimberly (Privacy/Trust) to propose contract language and attach our ops proof template. I'll get a redline back to you by EOD tomorrow.

Next step: can you confirm whether Crescent needs "no-derivatives" in addition to no-training (i.e., any derivative dataset or model artefact rights)? If so, we'll include explicit derivative/access prohibitions.

Thanks — Ben
Ben Carter
Account Executive, Enterprise
Redwood Inference
[attachment referenced: Ops_NoTrain_Proof_Template.pdf]

From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
To: Olga Petrov <olga.petrov@redwood.ai>, Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.ai>
Date: Wed, 30 Sep 2026 08:42:00 +0100
Subject: Fwd: DPA questions — Crescent Health (no-learn + EU routing)

Folks — forwarding Crescent's request. Key asks: signed no-training commitment, operational evidence (attestations/proofflow), EU routing + subprocessor list, and KMS residency options.

Attached: Crescent_DPA_Redlines_v1.docx (their redline), Ops_NoTrain_Proof_Template.pdf (our ops template).

Specific asks from Crescent:
- Confirmed line-item they want: "Provider shall not use Customer Data to improve, fine-tune, or train Provider models, nor shall Provider create derivative datasets from Customer Data."
- They want a short appendix listing subprocessors that will see content for routing reasons.
- They asked for a doc describing how we produce operational evidence (logs, attestation letters, KMS residency guarantees) suitable for their security review.

Olga — can you propose a concise DPA amendment (1-2 sentences + appendix reference) and any legal caveats re: subcontractor obligations?
Kimberly — can you confirm what we can produce as evidence for the EU routing claim (sftp/on-prem export options, ephemeral KV cache retention times, and the standard attestation language)?

Thanks — Ben
[Forwarded message below]

---------- Forwarded message ---------
From: Emma Gray <emma.gray@crescenthealth.io>
Date: Tue, 29 Sep 2026 13:15:00 +0100
Subject: DPA questions — no-learning commitment and EU residency
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>

(Original message above)

From: Olga Petrov <olga.petrov@redwood.ai>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>, Emma Gray <emma.gray@crescenthealth.io>
Cc: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.ai>
Date: Wed, 30 Sep 2026 10:02:00 +0100
Subject: Re: DPA questions — no-learning commitment and EU routing

Hi all — thanks Ben and Emma. I've reviewed Crescent's redline and drafted a short amendment that should be acceptable and tight enough for procurement. See summary below and next steps.

Proposed DPA amendment (summary):
- Add clause: "Provider will not use Customer Data to train, fine-tune, or otherwise improve Provider's models or derivatives thereof. Provider will not create derivative datasets from Customer Data for any internal or external model development purposes."
- Subprocessors: we will include an appendix listing named subprocessors and their processing regions; subcontractor flow-down language will be added so subprocessors must comply with the no-training clause for Crescent-bound requests.
- Operational evidence: Redwood will provide an Ops Evidence Pack containing (a) routing logs demonstrating EU-region endpoints for the requested account, (b) KV cache retention policy and scrub timeframes, (c) subprocessor attestations for relevant partners, and (d) optional KMS residency letters if customer elects customer-managed keys or Dedicated/Private deployment.

Caveats & options:
- For Hosted (standard) accounts we can provide attestation and routing logs; customer-managed KMS is only available for Dedicated/Private. If Crescent needs explicit onshore KMS for hosted, we'd need to discuss Dedicated/Private or a contractual SLA exception.
- The clause above is intentionally narrow (training/fine-tune/derivative creation). It does not prevent standard monitoring/telemetry used for service health (we can add a carve-out sentence describing that telemetry is used only for reliability and will be pseudonymized).

Next steps:
- Ben: share the amendment text below with Crescent and attach Ops_NoTrain_Proof_Template.pdf.
- Emma: confirm whether Crescent requires the "no-derivatives" phrasing (we included it) and whether you need KMS residency for hosted or plan to pursue Dedicated/Private.

Full amendment text (copy/pasteable):
"Provider shall not use Customer Data to train, fine-tune, or otherwise improve Provider's models. Provider shall not create derivative datasets from Customer Data for model training or distribution. Provider shall require all subprocessors who process Customer Data on behalf of Provider to comply with these restrictions and shall furnish an appendix identifying such subprocessors and their processing locations. Provider shall produce, upon Customer request, operational evidence of routing and processing locations and subprocessor attestations in the form of logs and signed attestations."

I'll also prepare a one-page Ops Evidence Pack that maps to their security checklist. Happy to join a call with Crescent Legal if they want to walk through the evidence.

Olga Petrov
Senior Counsel, Privacy & Security
Redwood Inference

[Attachment: Ops_NoTrain_Proof_Template.pdf attached to thread]

title: Post-demo: Performance checkpoints & p95/p99 acceptance for hosted vs incumbent
preview:
From: Avery Johnson <avery@redwood.com>
To: Sara Patel <sara@epsilonanalytics.com>, Epsilon Analytics Platform <infra@epsilonanalytics.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>, Jonas Weber <jonas@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 27 Sep 2026 15:12:00 -0700
Subject: Post-demo: Performance checkpoints & p95/p99 acceptance for hosted vs incumbent

Hi Sara / team,

Thanks again for taking time today — nice demo and good discussion about your production envelope. Quick recap from our side and suggested next steps so we stay aligned on what a minimal POC needs to prove out.

Key performance targets we heard today (please correct if I mis-stated):
- Current provider observed (your ops): p95 ≈ 180 ms, p99 ≈ 620 ms (end-to-end including client egress).
- Epsilon target for go/no-go: p95 <= 120 ms, p99 <= 400 ms during steady-state traffic; higher spikes tolerated briefly (e.g., during deploys).
- Streaming: you expect up to 50 concurrent long-poll streams during peak window, with bursts to ~120 concurrent for short windows.
- Throughput: baseline 250 QPS with 10:1 read/write mix on streaming session opens; peak 600 QPS shaped across regions.

Clarifying items we still need from you to scope the POC:
1) Representative payloads (sample requests + average token length + max token length).
2) Traffic profile: 1-minute, 5-minute, and 1-hour envelopes (we'll use these to create the load shape).
3) Any client-side network constraints (e.g., strict idle timeouts) and a small sample of logs showing current provider tail behavior.

What Redwood will do in the POC:
- Run a hosted API test against your sample traffic shape (end-to-end instrumented) and record p50/p95/p99, connection churn, and concurrency behavior.
- Enable prefix/KV caching for repeated tokens where appropriate to measure cost/latency improvements.
- Provide a short runbook (attached) for the canary and a CSV with preliminary latencies.

Next steps / proposed timeline:
- You share payloads & traffic profile by Wed, Sep 29.
- Redwood runs an initial 2-hour smoke with the traffic profile week of Oct 4; share results within 24 hours.
- If smoke looks good, run a 48-hour canary (Oct 6–8) and validate p95/p99 during peak windows.

Attachments: poc-runbook-epsilon.pdf (overview + metrics we will collect), redwood-hosted-performance-summary.md (short primer), latency_matrix_v2.csv (example output format).

If this aligns, I’ll lock the test window and kick Ben / Jonas on the setup.

Thanks — Avery

--
Avery Johnson
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
avery@redwood.com | +1 415-555-0147



From: Sara Patel <sara@epsilonanalytics.com>
To: Avery Johnson <avery@redwood.com>
Cc: Tom Liu <tom.liu@epsilonanalytics.com>, Epsilon Analytics Platform <infra@epsilonanalytics.com>
Date: Mon, 27 Sep 2026 17:03:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Post-demo: Performance checkpoints & p95/p99 acceptance for hosted vs incumbent

Thanks Avery — this is great, and mostly correct. A few clarifications from our side: 

- The numbers I quoted are indeed end-to-end (client -> provider -> client). The 620ms p99 is what our SREs see from synthetic checks; our production traces (customer traffic) show a broader tail to ~900ms during peak deploy windows.
- For the target: p95 <= 120ms is aspirational across the APIs we rely on; p99 <= 400ms is a firm requirement for our interactive workflows (customer-facing). For background jobs we can accept higher tail.
- Streaming specifics: long-lived streaming sessions are primarily for our real-time annotation product. Concurrency estimate is 40–60 typical, and yes we saw spikes to ~110 during promotions. Idle timeouts are 90s on our gateway.

We will provide the payload samples and the 1m/5m/1h traffic envelopes by Tue EOD (Sep 29). We can also export a 24-hour trace window that shows the deploy spike — will that be helpful?

Question for Redwood: can hosted API runs capture the end-to-end perspective (i.e., include client-side RTT and Gateway time)? We want the POC to reflect our real user experience, not just server compute time.

Appreciate the clear timeline — we’re aligned to the Oct 4 smoke and Oct 6–8 canary window pending the sample artifacts.

Best,
Sara

--
Sara Patel
Head of Platform, Epsilon Analytics
sara@epsilonanalytics.com

On Mon, Sep 27, 2026 at 15:12 Avery Johnson <avery@redwood.com> wrote:
> Hi Sara / team,
> 
> Thanks again for taking time today — nice demo and good discussion about your production envelope. Quick recap from our side and suggested next steps so we stay aligned on what a minimal POC needs to prove out.
> 
> [message truncated for thread]

From: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
To: Avery Johnson <avery@redwood.com>, Jonas Weber <jonas@redwood.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>, Sara Patel <sara@epsilonanalytics.com>
Date: Tue, 28 Sep 2026 09:18:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Post-demo: Performance checkpoints & p95/p99 acceptance for hosted vs incumbent

Looping in a short technical plan from our side. I’ll own the test harness and measurement methodology. High-level notes: 

1) Measurement scope: we will measure true end-to-end from a Redwood agent placed in the same region as your gateway (we can also run a small set of clients in your VPC if you prefer). Our reported p95/p99 will include TCP handshake + client network RTT + server processing + streaming flush latency so it should mirror your synthetic checks.

2) Traffic shaping: we’ll model the burst profile you provided (1m/5m/1h) and also a deploy-spike synthetic run to reproduce tail behavior.

3) Concurrency / streaming: we’ll validate with 50 concurrent persistent SSE streams and then stress to 120 for short durations to observe tail and connection churn. We’ll report session creation success rate and time-to-first-token.

4) Quantization & cache controls: for the hosted run we’ll run two modes — default FP16 quantized variant and a cached-path run to show delta on p95. This will let you see unit economics vs latency tradeoffs.

I attached a draft latency matrix (latency_matrix_v2.csv) with columns we’ll produce (timestamp, test-phase, p50,p95,p99,err_rate,concurrency). I’ll also publish the poc-runbook-epsilon.pdf to the drive link once we finalize small tweaks.

Ben

--
Ben Carter
Senior Solutions Engineer, Redwood Inference
ben@redwood.com | +1 650-555-0224



From: Avery Johnson <avery@redwood.com>
To: Sara Patel <sara@epsilonanalytics.com>, Tom Liu <tom.liu@epsilonanalytics.com>, Epsilon Analytics Platform <infra@epsilonanalytics.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>, Jonas Weber <jonas@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 29 Sep 2026 11:03:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Post-demo: Performance checkpoints & p95/p99 acceptance for hosted vs incumbent

Thanks Sara — great, we’ll proceed on the timeline. Quick confirmation items before we lock the window: 

- Can you upload the sample payloads and the 1m/5m/1h envelopes to the Drive link above (or share via S3) by EOD Tue (Sep 29)?
- Please include a 1-hour trace that contains the deploy spike if possible — that will be helpful to reproduce the tail.
- Sara: confirm that the 90s gateway idle timeout is fixed; we may add a short keep-alive in the POC client to avoid premature disconnects.

Proposed schedule: Oct 4 — 2hr smoke run (San Francisco region); Oct 6–8 — 48hr canary covering your peak window. We’ll send a calendar invite for the demo results review after the smoke (tentative: Oct 5 10:00 AM PT).

If all good I’ll share the invite and Ben will post the initial latency_matrix_v2.csv after the smoke.

Appreciate it — looking forward to demonstrating parity (and hopefully improvements) vs your incumbent.

Avery

--
Avery Johnson
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
avery@redwood.com | +1 415-555-0147

Attachments referenced: poc-runbook-epsilon.pdf (application/pdf), latency_matrix_v2.csv (text/csv), redwood-hosted-performance-summary.md (text/markdown)

title: Cohort stipend: eligibility & split-invoicing request
preview:
From: Ana Morales <ana@foundrylabs.io>
To: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Thu, 24 Sep 2026 15:10:00 -0700
Subject: Pilot cohort — stipend application and invoicing question

Hi Kimberly,

Thanks again for the product walkthrough last week — Foundry Labs is excited about moving a small cohort of 8 teams into a 12-week pilot. Two quick questions from our finance/ops side before we sign:

1) We typically ask sponsors to provide a $10k cohort stipend applied as credits across participating startups. Is Redwood able to accept that stipend as a prepaid credit that can be allocated across multiple customer accounts or will it need to be invoiced to Foundry Labs as a single line item?

2) Our accounting needs the ability to split the invoice into a portion for the program fee (billed to Foundry Labs) and the stipend (billed to our fiscal sponsor). Can Redwood support split invoicing, or do we need a consolidated invoice that we reallocate internally?

If helpful, I can share the draft SOW and our billing contact (contracts@foundrylabs.io). We’re hoping to close the paperwork this week so teams can onboard next month.

Appreciate any flexibility you have here — 
Ana
Program Lead | Foundry Labs
Attachment(s): Foundry_SOW_Draft_v2.pdf


From: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ana Morales <ana@foundrylabs.io>
Date: Fri, 25 Sep 2026 09:03:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Pilot cohort — stipend application and invoicing question

Hi Ana — great to hear Foundry is moving forward. Short answers below, followed by next steps.

1) Prepaid stipend as credits: Yes, we can apply a prepaid stipend to an account as a prepaid credit balance. For allocations across multiple internal customer accounts (i.e., the individual startups), we need an explicit allocation table from Foundry (CSV) that lists Redwood account IDs or partner-proxy account emails. We’ll apply the credit to the accounts once the SOW is countersigned and payment is received.

2) Split invoicing: We can support split invoicing in two ways — (A) one invoice with line-level bill-to that we route to separate payers (requires RevOps approval), or (B) two invoices (one for program fee billed to Foundry, one for stipend billed to your fiscal sponsor). Option B is usually faster from a billing perspective.

Next steps I propose:
- Please send the SOW to contracts@redwoodinference.com and CC me.
- If you want option B (two invoices), share the fiscal sponsor details and billing address.
- I'll loop in RevOps to confirm the timing and any ledger constraints.

I’m looping in Ben (Partnerships) and Lauren (RevOps) on this thread so we can get final confirmation quickly.

Best,
Kimberly Park
Head of Partnerships | Redwood Inference


From: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>, Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwoodinference.com>, Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Fri, 25 Sep 2026 09:05:00 -0700
Subject: Fwd: Pilot cohort — stipend application and invoicing question

Team — forwarding Foundry Labs’ ask. TL;DR: 8-team cohort, Foundry wants a $10k cohort stipend applied as prepaid credits across the participating startups + split invoicing (program fee to Foundry, stipend to fiscal sponsor).

Key points and asks:
- Can RevOps issue two invoices (program fee -> Foundry Labs; stipend -> fiscal sponsor) and confirm timeline for applying credits post-payment?
- If two invoices are not feasible, what are the blockers to line-level bill-to routing?
- Ben: do you want us to accept the stipend as a Redwood-managed prepaid (we’ll allocate credits once they provide account IDs) or insist they route stipend through the program fee and reallocate internally?

Attached: Foundry_SOW_Draft_v2.pdf (they sent it). I’ll make introductions to contracts@foundrylabs.io once we have the invoicing approach.

Thanks — need an internal yes/no today if possible — onboarding window tight.

— Kimberly


From: Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwoodinference.com>
To: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwoodinference.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>, Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Mon, 28 Sep 2026 11:22:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Fwd: Pilot cohort — stipend application and invoicing question

Kimberly/Ben/Neha —

We can support Option B (two invoices). Conditions and notes below:

- Stipend invoice ("Cohort Stipend - Foundry Labs Fiscal Sponsor") can be issued to the fiscal sponsor with a 30-day payment term. Once payment clears, accounting will generate prepaid credit entries and we’ll send a CSV template for allocations.
- Program fee invoice issued to Foundry Labs as normal (separate GL).
- For the prepaid-credit allocation: we need the allocation CSV at least 5 business days after payment date to process credits before onboarding. Template attached: cohort-stipend-calculation.xlsx.
- If Foundry wants us to allocate credits across accounts we don't already administer, they must provide either (a) Redwood account IDs or (b) verified contact emails for each startup; otherwise credits will be applied to a single partner-managed account and it's up to Foundry to redistribute.
- No change to contract terms needed beyond listing the billing recipients and identifying the stipend line item.

I’ll prepare the two invoice templates and a short billing schedule. Once they confirm fiscal sponsor billing address and the SOW is countersigned, we can issue invoices same day.

Lauren Bishop
Revenue Operations Manager | Redwood Inference
Attachment(s): cohort-stipend-calculation.xlsx


From: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ana Morales <ana@foundrylabs.io>, contracts@foundrylabs.io
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>, Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwoodinference.com>, Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, 29 Sep 2026 10:05:00 -0700
Subject: Confirming invoicing path & next steps for Foundry cohort

Hi Ana / Foundry contracts team —

Thanks for the quick back-and-forth. Per RevOps (Lauren) we can proceed with two invoices: one for the program fee billed to Foundry Labs and a second invoice for the $10,000 cohort stipend billed to your fiscal sponsor. Workflow and next steps below:

1) Please countersign the attached SOW (Foundry_SOW_Draft_v2.pdf) and return to contracts@foundrylabs.io.
2) Confirm fiscal sponsor legal name, billing address, and billing email for the stipend invoice.
3) Once payment for the stipend invoice clears, we will:
   - Issue prepaid credit entries for $10,000 total.
   - Send our allocation CSV template (attached) — please populate with Redwood account IDs or verified startup contact emails (we need this 5 business days after payment).
4) Expected timing: invoices issued same day SOW is countersigned; credits applied within 3 business days after payment + allocation CSV receipt.

Attachments: Foundry_SOW_Draft_v2.pdf, cohort-stipend-calculation.xlsx

If you prefer we can also accept payment by ACH (we’ll send bank details) or a hosted card link; let me know which your team prefers.

Ben/Neha: happy to let you handle kickoff post-payment — I’ll schedule the partner onboarding call once the allocation CSV is in.

Cheers,
Kimberly Park

Subject: Re: Pilot cohort — stipend application and invoicing question

Hi Ana — great to hear Foundry is moving forward. Short answers below, followed by next steps.

1) Prepaid stipend as credits: Yes, we can apply a prepaid stipend to an account as a prepaid credit balance. For allocations across multiple internal customer accounts (i.e., the individual startups), we need an explicit allocation table from Foundry (CSV) that lists Redwood account IDs or partner-proxy account emails. We’ll apply the credit to the accounts once the SOW is countersigned and payment is received.

2) Split invoicing: We can support split invoicing in two ways — (A) one invoice with line-level bill-to that we route to separate payers (requires RevOps approval), or (B) two invoices (one for program fee billed to Foundry, one for stipend billed to your fiscal sponsor). Option B is usually faster from a billing perspective.

Next steps I propose:
- Please send the SOW to contracts@redwoodinference.com and CC me.
- If you want option B (two invoices), share the fiscal sponsor details and billing address.
- I'll loop in RevOps to confirm the timing and any ledger constraints.

I’m looping in Ben (Partnerships) and Lauren (RevOps) on this thread so we can get final confirmation quickly.

Best,
Kimberly Park
Head of Partnerships | Redwood Inference

- Can RevOps issue two invoices (program fee -> Foundry Labs; stipend -> fiscal sponsor) and confirm timeline for applying credits post-payment?

Attached: Foundry_SOW_Draft_v2.pdf (they sent it). I’ll make introductions to contracts@foundrylabs.io once we have the invoicing approach.

— Kimberly

- No change to contract terms needed beyond listing the billing recipients and identifying the stipend line item.

title: Post-call: staggered technical deep-dive — three-shift scheduling options
preview:
From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>
To: Marta Silva <marta.silva@medsys.io>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 28 Sep 2026 08:15:00 -0400
Subject: Post-call: staggered technical deep-dive — three-shift scheduling options

Hi Marta — thanks again for a great discovery call this morning. Quick recap and suggested next steps so we can lock in a tech deep-dive: 

Summary from call:
- Use case: secure LLM inference for clinical-note summarization and search across patient records (PHI-in-scope).
- Primary constraints: on-prem/private deployment requirement in EMEA, strict audit logging, SLA window for evening batch processing.
- Success metric: 95th pct latency < 300ms for user-facing queries; embedding throughput for nightly jobs.

Suggested next step: 60–90 minute technical deep-dive with our infra and security engineers to walk architecture, compliance checklist, and a short sandbox demo. To make this work for your global teams we sketched a "three-shift" rotating schedule (US / EU / APAC) so stakeholders can attend at reasonable hours on a recurring cadence. Attached is a simple availability grid and one-page proposal for rotating slots.

Proposed windows (first pass):
- US-friendly slot: Tue 10:00–11:30 ET (good for east coast teams)
- EU-friendly slot: Wed 14:00–15:30 CET (aligns with EMEA technical owners)
- APAC-friendly slot: Thu 09:00–10:30 SGT (morning APAC block)

Options for cadence — please indicate preferred:
1) Weekly rotation: same week alternate region (fast turnaround for POC teams).
2) Fixed-week blocks: e.g., two weeks per region then rotate (less context switching).
3) Monthly rotating slot for exec/architect review, weekly region-specific deep-dive for engineering.

Next actions: can you confirm which cadence you prefer and any absolute blackout dates (internal compliance review next month, etc.)? I put a Calendly link in the follow-up for each slot and attached an availability grid (availability_grid_proposal.xlsx).

Thanks — happy to adjust.

Best,
Ava Chen
Senior Solutions Engineer | Redwood Inference
ava.chen@redwood.com
+1 (415) 555-0119

---
Attachment: availability_grid_proposal.xlsx (spreadsheet with sample windows and timezone conversions)

- Blackout: the week of Oct 11 is problematic for our R&D sprint-ending testing.

title: Deal-weighted GPU runway: weekly scorecard & prioritization
preview:
From: Chloe Martin <chloe@redwood.com>
To: Jordan Blake <jordan@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.com>, Laura Bennett <laura@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus@redwood.com>
Cc: Sam Patel <sam.patel@novaapps.com>
Date: Tue, 28 Sep 2026 15:12:00 -0700
Subject: Deal-weighted GPU runway: weekly scorecard & prioritization

Team — attaching this week’s deal-weighted scorecard and the pipeline snapshot as of EOD 2026-09-27. Key ask: we need a quick prioritization on which opportunities to seed with reserved capacity versus burst credits for the next 30–60 day runway.

High-level notes (see attachment for per-deal numbers):
- NovaApps (DEAL-87654): high-touch pilot + PoV. Sales requests 24 reserved A100-equivalent slots for 6 weeks starting Nov 1. Pipeline indicates potential MC close in Q1.
- Orion.ai and Helio Labs are lower-touch; recommend burst-only unless they top the commit list.
- Risk/mitigation: we currently have 72 reserved slots unassigned across east/us and west/us pools.

Asks by EOD today:
1) Jordan — confirm sales priority order for these top 6 deals and whether NovaApps is top 1.
2) Karthik — confirm feasibility of a 24-slot reservation in west/us for Nov 1 and any infra frictions (images, quantization variants).
3) Laura — please flag any financial guardrails if we move 24 slots to a pilot reserve (impact to committed spend and how quickly we can reallocate).

Attachments: deal_weighted_scorecard_Q4.xlsx (spreadsheet), pipeline_snapshot_20260927.csv (csv)

Thanks,
Chloe Martin
Sales Operations Manager
Redwood Inference


From: Jordan Blake <jordan@redwood.com>
To: Chloe Martin <chloe@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.com>, Laura Bennett <laura@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus@redwood.com>
Cc: Sam Patel <sam.patel@novaapps.com>
Date: Tue, 28 Sep 2026 15:37:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Deal-weighted GPU runway: weekly scorecard & prioritization

On Tue, Sep 28, 2026 at 3:12 PM Chloe Martin <chloe@redwood.com> wrote:
> Team — attaching this week’s deal-weighted scorecard and the pipeline snapshot as of EOD 2026-09-27. Key ask: we need a quick prioritization on which opportunities to seed with reserved capacity versus burst credits for the next 30–60 day runway.
>
> Asks by EOD today:
> 1) Jordan — confirm sales priority order for these top 6 deals and whether NovaApps is top 1.

Chloe — NovaApps is top priority for Q4 outreach and pilot support. Sam’s team is sensitive to performance jitter; they’ve asked for reserved capacity to meet latency SLOs during the pilot.

Priority I’d propose (top 4 to consider for reserves):
1. NovaApps (DEAL-87654) — yes, top 1
2. Helio Labs (DEAL-88112) — medium reserve if capacity permits
3. Orion.ai (DEAL-87221) — burst preferred
4. AtlasEdge (DEAL-86302) — low priority for reserve

If infra can commit the 24 slots for NovaApps on Nov 1, I’ll push procurement to sign a 6-week pilot MSA and convert to a commit if usage hits thresholds.

-Jordan
Senior Account Executive
Redwood Inference


From: Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.com>
To: Chloe Martin <chloe@redwood.com>, Jordan Blake <jordan@redwood.com>, Laura Bennett <laura@redwood.com>
Cc: Marcus Lin <marcus@redwood.com>, Sam Patel <sam.patel@novaapps.com>
Date: Tue, 28 Sep 2026 16:05:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Deal-weighted GPU runway: weekly scorecard & prioritization

Chloe / team — quick infrastructure feasibility note.

- We have an unassigned west/us physical pool of 40 A100-equivalent slots and east/us pool of 32. After scheduled maintenance and the expected burst window for next week (internal experiments), realistic available reserveable capacity for Nov 1 is ~48 slots across regions.
- Reserving 24 slots for NovaApps in west/us is feasible but:
  * We need provisioning of the customer image and quantized model variant by Oct 15 to validate start-of-runway perf.
  * If NovaApps requires pre-warming (long KV cache lifetimes) that reduces burst headroom — we’ll need to set a strict autoscale/fallback policy.
- Recommendation: approve 24-slot reserve in west/us, but mark it as convertible reserve: if NovaApps converts to a committed annual, we’ll shift to a reserved contract; otherwise, release after 6 weeks.

Action items for infra (me):
- Reserve 24 west/us slots tentatively; hold until Laura signs off on budget movement.
- Create a tiny runbook for pre-warming and fallback to burst; link to OPS ticket when created.

Attachment: pipeline_snapshot_20260927.csv (used for region mapping)

Karthik Iyer
Head of Infrastructure
Redwood Inference

To: Jordan Blake <jordan@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.com>, Laura Bennett <laura@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus@redwood.com>
Cc: Sam Patel <sam.patel@novaapps.com>, procurement@novaapps.com
Date: Tue, 28 Sep 2026 17:40:00 -0700

Thanks all — quick readout and next steps: 

Decision for now:
- Approve a tentative 24-slot west/us reserve for NovaApps starting Nov 1, contingent on finance sign-off by Oct 8 and infra validation of image/quantization by Oct 15.

Action owners and deadlines:
- Laura (Finance): confirm budget movement and any required PO/process by Oct 8.
- Karthik (Infra): create OPS ticket and runbook for pre-warm/fallback; confirm slot reservation window by Oct 15.
- Jordan (Sales/AE): kick off procurement conversation with NovaApps and share target MSA terms; coordinate pilot start dates.
- Marcus (Product): advise if any model variants require special kernel selection; loop in runtime team if needed.

I’ll consolidate into a short mutual action plan doc and post the HubSpot action (87654). If finance greenlights, Karthik can mark the slots in the reserve ledger and we’ll schedule a 30-minute handoff call next Wed (Oct 13) if needed.
