title: MAP: Technical evaluation rundown, milestone owners & decision timeline
preview:
From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
To: Sana Ahmed <sana@brightbridge.health>; Tom Larkin <tom.larkin@brightbridge.health>
Cc: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.ai>, Grace O'Connor <grace.oconnor@redwood.ai>
Date: 2026-09-21T15:12:00-07:00
Subject: MAP: Technical evaluation rundown, milestone owners & decision timeline

Hi Sana / Tom —

Great to meet you both on the call today. As promised, attached is a first-pass MAP focused on the technical evaluation milestone so your team can track ownership and exit criteria. Quick recap of the ask from today and what I'm attaching: 

- Objective: confirm the model slate and acceptance criteria for your pilot (customer-facing summarization + secure PHI-handling flows).
- Target decision date (requested on call): Oct 22 2026 — we penciled this into the commercial cadence.
- What I attached: BrightBridge_MAP_DRAFT_v1.xlsx (MAP with milestone owners) and llm-benchmark-asks.md (detailed benchmark checklist).

Key technical milestone (what we want to lock down in the technical evaluation):
- Benchmark success criteria: semantic fidelity >= 85% on our sample prompts (F1-style human eval), hallucination rate < 3% in safety prompts, and no PHI leakage in 1000 synthetic patient records (zero high-severity events).
- Latency targets: P95 <= 350ms for 256-token completions in your west-us region under simulated 30 RPS sustained load; cold-start 99th percentile warnings documented.
- Cost target: inference unit cost target <= $0.0018 per 1k tokens (as a sensitivity goal) across the shortlisted models with standard quantization settings.
- Model shortlist (recommended for trials): Llama-3-70B (8-bit quant), Mistral-7B Instruct (fp16), and Redstone-13B-quant (our verified quantized variant).

Milestone owners (initial):
- BrightBridge: Technical lead (Tom) — run internal privacy checks and provide datasets for bench.
- Redwood: Karthik Iyer — benchmarking runs, cost/latency tuning recommendations.
- Redwood: Grace O'Connor — integration support (auth, logging, audit traces) + runbook for KV caching.
- Sales (me) — commercial alignment and rollout coordination.

Next steps I suggest by end of this week: 
1) Tom confirm the set of 12 representative prompts and the synthetic PHI corpus we agreed on (attaching format in llm-benchmark-asks.md).
2) BrightBridge sends creation access for a short-lived S3 bucket (read-only) or share Drive link with datasets.
3) Karthik to schedule first benchmark kickoff (we can do a 60-min run-through next Tue).

If that looks right I'll update the MAP sheet with the exact owners and the Oct 22 decision row so procurement can tie milestones to the PO process.

Thanks — talk soon.

—
Ben Carter
Enterprise Account Executive | Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.ai | +1 (415) 555-0192
Redwood Inference

Attachments: BrightBridge_MAP_DRAFT_v1.xlsx (spreadsheet), llm-benchmark-asks.md (text)

From: Grace O'Connor <grace.oconnor@redwood.ai>

Subject: Re: MAP: Technical evaluation rundown, milestone owners & decision timeline

Hi all — attaching the short security onboarding snippet below and dropping a Drive link with our audit log template. I'll put the full snippet into the MAP sheet as well.

Security onboarding (short):
- Keys & access: Redwood issues ephemeral API keys scoped to a single test run; keys expire after 24 hours by default. For Dedicated/Private deployments we support customer-managed KMS integration (AWS KMS / GCP KMS / HSM).
- Audit & traces: per-request headers are captured for debugging; request payloads are redacted for stored logs by default. We provide an exportable audit log (CSV/JSON) covering request id, timestamp, model variant, latency metrics, and outcome buckets.
- Data handling: inputs used for benchmarking can be kept ephemeral and are deleted after 30 days unless customer agrees to retain for regression monitoring. Retention policy can be set per MAP milestone.
- SOC2 artifacts & evidence: we'll share relevant SOC2 report extracts via an NDA'd secure link once procurement confirms the NDA scope.

Drive link: https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1aB_example_map_folder (contains the MAP draft, run harness README, and the audit-log-template.json stub).

Ben — I'll finish the short paragraph for the procurement/security section in the MAP and then you can circulate the updated spreadsheet to procurement.

Regards,
Grace O'Connor
Senior Solutions Engineer | Redwood Inference

Security onboarding (short):

- Audit & traces: per-request headers are captured for debugging; request payloads are redacted for stored logs by default. We provide an exportable audit log (CSV/JSON) covering request id, timestamp, model variant, latency metrics, and outcome buckets.

> Kiran —
>
> Thanks again for the demo last week. Attached is the mutual action plan (MAP) spreadsheet and an initial SOW (map-scope-v1.xlsx). High level asks / next steps:
>
> - Redwood to provide SOC2 summary and list of technical contacts by 09/24.

Thanks Ben — appreciate the clarity. A couple of constraints from our side that will affect the MAP timeline:

1) Our security team has requested SOC2 Type II evidence and a mapping of where PHI may be transiently present in logs; until we can satisfy that we cannot open standard procurement.
2) Our legal/compliance team needs to see data retention controls and an export/erasure playbook for the pilot.
3) We have an internal procurement freeze for platform purchases expiring 10/05 — if we cannot present a narrowly scoped pilot and SOW that avoids long-term retention, we likely will miss the 10/15 close.

Would Redwood consider a limited pilot ("escape hatch" lane) with: 1) masked data only, 2) 7-day retention for telemetry, and 3) an explicit audit log delivered to our security mailbox? If so, please send a short pilot SOW and redlines for our legal team.

Kiran Shah
Director, Digital Platforms | Skyward Health
kiran@skywardhealth.com | +1 (212) 555-0134


From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>
To: Kiran Shah <kiran@skywardhealth.com>

Date: Wed, 22 Sep 2026 14:30:00 -0700

Kiran —

Thanks for the flags; totally understand. I've sketched an approach below that we can move on immediately.

Interim acceptance lanes (proposal):

Lane A — Limited pilot (escape hatch):
- Scope: up to 3 concurrent users, masked input, telemetry stored for 7 days.
- Acceptance: smoke test scripts + 1 two-hour live session with your security team.
- Owner: Ben Carter (Sales). Target sign-off: 2026-09-30.

Lane B — Full MAP (normal close):
- Scope: full ingestion + production routing and metrics.
- Acceptance: evaluation plan (30-day SLA window) and functional sign-offs.
- Owner: Jonas Weber (Solutions Engineering). Target sign-off: 2026-10-12.

Escalation / fallbacks:
- If Lane A cannot be agreed by 09/30, escalate to Sales Director for exec-level trade-offs; proposed fallback close = 2026-11-01.
- Redwood will provide a security evidence inventory (attachment) and a 1-page data retention playbook for the pilot.

Attachments: pilot-sow.pdf, security-evidence-inventory.csv

Action items (short):
1) Laura: please run the evidence inventory for SOC2 items and confirm what we can share by 09/24.
2) Jonas: draft the limited-pilot test script and retention configuration (due 09/27).
3) Kiran: confirm whether the limited pilot approach is acceptable to legal / security by EOD 09/24.

If that timeline works I'll circulate a 1-page SOW for signature (DocuSign link) and spin up a sandbox instance for the pilot.

Best,
Ben

--
Ben Carter
Strategic Sales | Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwoodinference.com


From: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwoodinference.com>

- Risk summary: Lane A materially reduces our legal exposure (short retention, masked inputs) but will still require a simple DPA addendum and an audit log delivery mechanism.
- Controls we can promise by 09/24: SOC2 control mapping (redacted), encryption-at-rest note, telemetry retention TTL config.
- Open ask: legal needs a one-page indemnity carve-out for the pilot. That will likely add 2 business days to processing.

Recommendation:
1) Move forward with Lane A SOW today and include an explicit pilot end-date (10/06) so procurement counts it as short-term.
2) I will loop Legal (cc not present) for the one-page carve-out; if Legal asks for exec sign-off we should escalate to Sales Director.
3) Jonas to have the sandbox and test script ready by 09/27.

I will update the MAP spreadsheet with these lanes and ownerships (will push to the Drive link).

Laura Bennett

title: RFI: Isolated deployment evidence & operational commitments
preview:
From: Samuel Klein <samuel.klein@aegishealth.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
Date: 2026-09-18T09:12:00-07:00
Subject: RFI: evidence for isolated/on-prem deployment readiness

Hi Ben,

Thanks for the quick demo last week. Our security and procurement teams have a short RFI asking for concrete evidence that Redwood can run in an isolated/on-prem or VPC-with-strict-egress configuration for highly regulated workloads. Specifically they want the following artifacts and clarifications:

- Network topology diagram showing no outbound egress except approved jump hosts (if applicable).
- KMS/key management architecture (do you support HSM-backed keys and an offline rotation process?).
- Patch and update process for air-gapped nodes (how are patches delivered and verified?).
- Audit/log export capability (formats, retention, and whether logs are SIEM friendly).
- Proof of operations: an example runbook for emergency rollback and data-wipe procedures.

Can you confirm what you can provide and a realistic ETA? Our target is to complete the RFI for our internal board review by the end of next week.

Also let me know if you prefer we receive artifacts via SFTP, a signed bucket link, or a secure SharePoint upload.

Thanks—
Sam
Samuel Klein
Sr. Security Engineer, Aegis Health
samuel.klein@aegishealth.com


From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
To: Samuel Klein <samuel.klein@aegishealth.com>
Cc: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.ai>, Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa.ortiz@redwood.ai>
Date: 2026-09-18T11:47:00-07:00
Subject: Re: RFI: evidence for isolated/on-prem deployment readiness

Hi Sam —

Appreciate the clarity. We can provide a bundled evidence pack and a few follow-up items. High level commitments below, details attached.

What we can deliver (attached):
- Network topology and annotated diagram (network_topology_aegis.png)
- KMS architecture diagram and HSM integration notes (included in PDF)
- Sample ops runbook: emergency rollback + wipe playbook (ops_airgap_checklist_20260923.xlsx)
- Exportable audit/log samples in JSON/CEF format and description of the export API
- Attestation matrix describing who owns which operational step (Redwood vs customer)

Proposed ETA: compile and ship the initial redacted evidence bundle by EOD 2026-09-23. Full, unredacted artifacts (including sample logs) can be placed in a time-limited secure bucket once we agree on transfer method and an NDA addendum if required.

Questions for you:
- Preferred transfer method (SFTP, signed GCS/S3 link, or SharePoint/SecureBox)?
- Do you need HSM key material export, or just architecture and attestation that keys are HSM-backed?
- Any required retention windows or SIEM formats we should include specifically?

I’m looping in Laura (security & compliance) and Vanessa (solutions/ops) to assemble the bundle and confirm the technical answers.

Thanks,

Ben Carter
Account Executive — Enterprise | Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.ai | +1 (415) 555-0122

Attachments: redwood_isolated_deploy_evidence_bundle_20260923.pdf, network_topology_aegis.png, ops_airgap_checklist_20260923.xlsx


From: Samuel Klein <samuel.klein@aegishealth.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
Cc: Aegis Procurement <procurement@aegishealth.com>
Date: 2026-09-18T13:05:00-07:00
Subject: Re: RFI: evidence for isolated/on-prem deployment readiness

Ben —

Thanks. A couple of follow-ups from our security lead:

1) KMS: we require HSM-backed keys, and an API or offline mechanism to rotate keys without outbound calls to vendor-managed services. Does Redwood support customer-managed HSM (on-prem HSM or cloud HSM in customer VPC) and offline rotation workflows?

2) Patching: for truly air-gapped installs, how are binary updates delivered and verified? We need a reproducible hash verification and an option for an operator to approve a particular package prior to installation.

3) Logs: can you provide logs in JSON/CEF and a sample SIEM ingestion mapping? Also we need a one-off export with a CSV of auth and admin events for the last 90 days as part of our board review.

If you can confirm support (Y/N) for the above and firm dates for the artifacts, we’ll flag any gaps quickly.

Best,
Sam

title: Vendor tax treaty attestation and multi-entity PO splitting - Altair Tech
preview:
From: Marie Dubois <marie.dubois@altairtech.fr>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Cc: procurement@altairtech.com
Date: Mon, 21 Sep 2026 16:12:00 +0200
Subject: Onboarding: tax treaty proof + how to split POs across entities

Hi Ben,

Thanks for the quick onboarding welcome earlier. We're getting our finance team lined up to complete Redwood's supplier setup but have a couple of questions before we send documents:

1) Altair Tech has an EU-registered subsidiary (Altair Tech SARL, FR) and a US parent (Altair Tech, Inc). For services provided to Redwood, can we claim tax treaty relief for US-sourced withholding? If so, what exact documentation do you need?

2) Redwood's AP team asked whether POs need to be split by billing entity (EUR vs USD). Is there a standard mapping you use?

I've attached our internal tax declaration (AT-TAX-DECLARATION.pdf) so you can see what we typically provide. Procurement is looped in (procurement@altairtech.com) and will coordinate uploads.

Can you confirm the checklist and next steps so we can avoid payment delays?

Best,
Marie
Marie Dubois
Finance Manager, Altair Tech SARL
+33 1 23 45 67 89

--- Attachment: AT-TAX-DECLARATION.pdf (1 page, stub)


From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Marie Dubois <marie.dubois@altairtech.fr>
Cc: procurement@altairtech.com, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 21 Sep 2026 18:05:00 +0200
Subject: Re: Onboarding: tax treaty proof + how to split POs across entities

Hi Marie —

Thanks for the note and the attachment. Short answers first: yes, you can claim treaty relief in many cases, but we need documentation; and yes, our AP requires separate POs per billing/legal entity. Details below.

What we need from you to evaluate a tax treaty / withholding exemption:
- Certificate of Tax Residency (or equivalent local CFR) for the billing entity (scanned PDF)
- Completed supplier tax attestation form (we have a short form linked below)
- VAT registration number (for EU reverse-charge handling) and copy of a recent VAT invoice showing the VRN helps
- Bank details (IBAN + BIC) and legal registration number for the billing entity

For PO mapping:
- If Altair Tech SARL (FR) will invoice in EUR to Redwood EU entity, we'll issue a EUR PO to that legal entity.
- If Altair Tech, Inc invoices Redwood US, we'll issue a USD PO to the US entity.
- If a single invoice covers services for both customers / regions, we still need separate POs per Redwood billing entity and a single supplier-side invoice that references both PO numbers and splits lines by entity.

Next steps / links:
1) Please upload the CFR/Certificate of Residence and your VAT registration to our supplier form: https://redwood.app/supplier-form?token=ab12cd
2) I attached our onboarding checklist (redwood-supplier-onboarding-checklist.pdf) — use that to collate files.
3) Karthik (cc'd) will confirm any additional fields our tax team requires for treaty claims.

If you prefer, we can accept a DocuSign CFR or a notarized PDF — both are fine.

I can schedule a short 20-min call with procurement to walk through PO mapping if helpful.

Thanks,
Ben Carter
Enterprise Sales, Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.com | +1 415 555 0123

--- Attachment: redwood-supplier-onboarding-checklist.pdf (1 page, stub)


From: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>
To: Marie Dubois <marie.dubois@altairtech.fr>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Cc: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 22 Sep 2026 09:37:00 +0200
Subject: Required evidence for treaty withholding and invoice routing

Hi Marie,

Looping in a couple of specifics from Finance so you know exactly what we will accept to process a treaty claim without withholding:

Required for a US treaty claim (services paid by Redwood US):
- Certificate of Fiscal Residence (CFR) for the supplier entity issued by local tax authority (PDF) OR a W-8BEN-E with a valid part claiming treaty benefit and accompanying CFR.
- Signed tax treaty declaration (simple cover letter on company letterhead stating claim and effective period).
- Payment instructions must match the legal name on CFR/W-8 and bank account beneficiary.

For EU reverse-charge (services invoiced to Redwood EU entity):
- Valid VAT number (VRN) and VAT invoices that include VRN and reverse-charge notes.
- We accept national VRN validation screenshots or the VAT certificate.

Format and transmission:
- Preferred: upload to the supplier portal link Ben provided.
- Alternative: secure DocuSign envelope (we can send).

One more operational note on POs: to avoid PO reconciliation issues please include the Redwood PO number on every invoice line. If you plan to submit a single combined invoice for two Redwood entities, split the lines and include both PO numbers and billing entity postal addresses.

I've attached a Certificate of Fiscal Residence template (CFR-template.pdf) you can use if you need to request one from your tax advisor or local office.

Cheers,
Karthik Iyer
Head of Finance Operations, Redwood Inference
karthik.iyer@redwood.com | +1 415 555 0199

--- Attachment: CFR-template.pdf (stub)

Hi Ben / Karthik,

Thanks — that clarifies a lot. Two quick follow-ups from procurement before we upload:

1) If Altair SARL provides the CFR (French residence certificate) and a signed declaration, will Redwood set withholding to 0% automatically for subsequent invoices or do you re-validate each year?
2) Can you attach a sample invoice header that shows both PO numbers for split billing? Procurement asked for a visual example so their AP system can validate line mapping.

We'll prepare the CFR and signed letter. Also, please confirm whether Victoire (our AP lead) should send the DocuSign envelope or if Redwood will initiate.

Thanks,
Marie

Date: Wed, 23 Sep 2026 22:45:00 +0200
Subject: Re: Vendor tax treaty attestation and multi-entity PO splitting - Altair Tech

Hi Marie —

Answers below and next steps:

1) Withholding: once Finance receives and validates the CFR/W-8 (as applicable) we'll tag the supplier record and apply the treaty relief to future payments. We do annual re-validation for residency documents (typically a rolling 12-month validity) or sooner if tax authorities issue updated certificates. If documentation is not present, standard withholding rules apply. (Note: withholding rates depend on treaty and service type; your tax counsel can confirm the exact treaty rate for Altair).

2) Sample invoice: attached is a quick mockup showing two line items, each referencing a different Redwood PO and billing entity. That layout works in our AP system: invoice-level header with supplier legal name + one invoice referencing multiple PO numbers with per-line PO references.

3) DocuSign: we can initiate the DocuSign envelope for the CFR/tax declaration to simplify signatures. If you prefer to upload directly, use the supplier form link. Let us know which you prefer and we will send the DocuSign link to Victoire.

Proposed timeline:
- Today/Tomorrow: Altair uploads CFR + signed attestation (or signs DocuSign)
- Finance validation: 2–4 business days after receipt
- First EUR PO issued for Altair SARL: once vendor record is activated (expected within 5 business days)

I'll follow up with a calendar invite for a 20-min handoff if that helps. Thanks for moving quickly on this — should be straightforward.

Ben

Looping in a couple of specifics from Finance so you know exactly what we will accept to process a treaty claim without withholding:

Required for a US treaty claim (services paid by Redwood US):
- Certificate of Fiscal Residence (CFR) for the supplier entity issued by local tax authority (PDF) OR a W-8BEN-E with a valid part claiming treaty benefit and accompanying CFR.
- Signed tax treaty declaration (simple cover letter on company letterhead stating claim and effective period).
- Payment instructions must match the legal name on CFR/W-8 and bank account beneficiary.

For EU reverse-charge (services invoiced to Redwood EU entity):
- Valid VAT number (VRN) and VAT invoices that include VRN and reverse-charge notes.
- We accept national VRN validation screenshots or the VAT certificate.

Format and transmission:
- Preferred: upload to the supplier portal link Ben provided.
- Alternative: secure DocuSign envelope (we can send).

One more operational note on POs: to avoid PO reconciliation issues please include the Redwood PO number on every invoice line. If you plan to submit a single combined invoice for two Redwood entities, split the lines and include both PO numbers and billing entity postal addresses.

title: ClearPay: Dedicated pool exhaustion causing 5xx/latency — urgent hold and mitigation
preview:
From: Ellen Park <ellen.park@clearpay.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Cc: ClearPay SRE <sre@clearpay.com>
Date: Tue, 22 Sep 2026 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Re: production errors and latency spike - urgent

Ben —

We started seeing a sustained latency increase and 5xx errors hitting our checkout path beginning ~08:45 PT. Tokens are queued in our service and our side has verified token-level timeouts coming back with 502s from your dedicated pool. This is impacting authorization calls and is causing failed checkouts.

Quick summary:
- 5xx rate: ~7% of requests from 08:45–09:00, now ~5% steady
- P95 latency for /v1/generate: 2.1s -> 14s
- We are on a reserved capacity plan (contract/commit DEAL-7743); this is production traffic.

Attached is a short log sample showing the failed responses and token queueing (clearpay-5xx-sample-logs-20260922.txt). We need an immediate remediation path and a clear ETA for either: (a) restoring latency to baseline, or (b) a validated temporary failover that preserves correctness.

We are available for a call right now. Please escalate if this needs leadership sign-off.

—Ellen
Ellen Park
Head of Platform, ClearPay


From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Ellen Park <ellen.park@clearpay.com>
Cc: Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.com>, Jonas Weber <jonas.weber@redwood.com>, Naomi Feldman <naomi.feldman@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 22 Sep 2026 09:22:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: production errors and latency spike - urgent

Ellen —

Thank you for the heads-up and the logs. I’m escalating this as a top priority. Quick acknowledgement and next steps from our side:

1) Triage in progress — I’ve looped in Jonas (ops) and Monica (CS) to coordinate with you.
2) Short-term options we can execute now:
   - Route ClearPay traffic to the light-weight quantized model variant in the same family (lower CPU/GPU per-token) — ~10–15 min to activate if you approve a temporary functional fallback.
   - Apply a conservative max_tokens cap per request (suggest 256) to reduce KV cache pressure — can be enforced immediately from our API gateway for your API key.
   - Initiate emergency burst provisioning from a neighboring dedicated pool (requires ops + finance/leadership approval if it exceeds reserved commit).
3) Monica will pull the mutual action plan and confirm a time-boxed remediation window; Jonas will provide a root-cause hypothesis and an estimated ETA for a code/config remediation.

Are you available for a 20-minute bridge at 09:40 PT? If you’d prefer, we can flip the traffic to the quantized variant while we investigate. Let me know which you prefer — failover now or config throttle + investigation.

I’ll keep you posted in-line.

Best,
Ben Carter
Senior Sales Lead, Redwood Inference


From: Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Jonas Weber <jonas.weber@redwood.com>, Naomi Feldman <naomi.feldman@redwood.com>
Cc: Ellen Park <ellen.park@clearpay.com>
Date: Tue, 22 Sep 2026 09:35:00 -07:00
Subject: RE: production errors and latency spike - urgent

Team —

Thanks Ben. Quick customer-facing plan (for Ellen):
- Offer immediate failover to quantized variant for the highest-throughput route (authorization) for a 45-minute window while we validate stability.
- Apply token cap (256) and remove non-critical system prompts to cut per-request token usage.
- Convene a post-incident MOP within 48 hours to confirm what to revert and whether to request temporary capacity increases.

Internal asks:
- Jonas: can you confirm the time to enable the quantized variant and the risk matrix?
- Naomi: please prepare an impact note describing whether a temporary burst from another dedicated pool will be chargeable and the expected cost ballpark. Monica will also open the MAP (attached: mutual_action_plan_clearpay_20260922.xlsx).

Ellen — would you accept the 45-minute trial of the quantized variant so we can observe error rates? If yes, we’ll schedule the bridge and perform the flip in the next 10–15 minutes.

—Monica
Customer Success Manager, Redwood Inference


From: Jonas Weber <jonas.weber@redwood.com>
To: Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Naomi Feldman <naomi.feldman@redwood.com>
Cc: Vivek Kulkarni <vivek.kulkarni@redwood.com>, Ellen Park <ellen.park@clearpay.com>
Date: Tue, 22 Sep 2026 13:08:00 -07:00
Subject: RE: production errors and latency spike - urgent

Short technical readout and recommended immediate actions:

Observed (prelim):
- Dedicated pool servicing ClearPay is hitting sustained GPU utilization > 93% on a subset of nodes and our scheduler is dropping queued requests when KV cache thrash spikes.
- Long-context invoices and a small percentage of very large batch generation calls are creating disproportionate KV cache churn.

Immediate mitigations we can apply in sequence (low -> higher risk):
1) API gateway cap: enforce max_tokens=256 for ClearPay key (low risk, immediate) — ETA: 3–7 minutes.
2) Flip route to quantized M-variant (compatibility check passed) — ETA: 12–18 minutes; expected P95 improvement 40–60% for this workload.
3) Enable aggressive prefix/KV caching for stable routes (requires brief model-config push) — ETA: 25–40 minutes.
4) Emergency cross-pool burst: provision + route overflow to adjacent dedicated pool (will exceed their reservation and incur overage). This needs leadership sign-off (Vivek) for costs and temporary capacity change — ETA after approval: ~20 minutes to provision and warm.

Requesting: Vivek — please authorize up to one hour of burst capacity (we will capture exact cost after the window). Naomi, please confirm whether finance pre-approval is required to trigger a short-duration burst to avoid manual billing blockers.

If we perform 1+2 now we should see the 5xx rate drop under 0.5% and P95 latency move back toward baseline within the 45-minute observation window.

I’m standing by on the bridge to implement.

-Jonas
Senior Ops Engineer, Redwood Inference

title: BrightFolio evaluation pack & customer data sample request
preview:
From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Elena Torres <elena.torres@brightfolio.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-09-19T16:12:00-07:00
Subject: BrightFolio evaluation pack & customer data sample request

Hi Elena —

Great to meet you and the team on Friday. Quick recap and what we need to get a tight eval moving:

What we discussed:
- Primary use case: customer support summarization + triage for B2B invoices and claims.
- Important constraints: PII redaction, latency target ~250ms median for short summaries, tolerate occasional longer generations for complex claims.
- Success metric anchors: F1 on entity extraction > 0.88, median latency < 300ms, and low hallucination rate per our eval script.

What would unblock the eval on our side (requested artifacts):
1) Representative prompt set (10–30 prompts per workflow: short invoice, multi-paragraph claim, tricky edge case). A single CSV is fine.
2) 50–200 anonymized sample records we can use for benchmarking. If you prefer, we can accept a 1% random sample exported to JSONL with a redaction column.
3) Any existing business rules / do-not-respond lists (for redaction and safety alignment).

Redaction guidance we suggest (optional): replace PII with tokens like [REDACTED_NAME], keep dates as YYYY-MM-DD if you need them preserved, and provide a separate map if you want reversible masking during later stages. If you cannot provide direct samples, a small synthetic set that preserves structure will also work.

Attached: brightfolio_eval_template.csv (prompt columns + expected label columns) and a short benchmark summary we ran on a sample workload.

Next steps: if you can share items #1–#3 by EOD Thursday (2026-09-23), Marissa and I will run a focused model-fit pass and deliver a short playbook (recommended models, quantization settings, and a suggested rollout plan) within 72 hours.

Thanks —
Ben
Account Executive | Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.com | +1 (415) 555-0124



From: Elena Torres <elena.torres@brightfolio.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Cc: BrightFolio Data Team <data-team@brightfolio.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-09-20T09:18:00-07:00
Subject: Re: BrightFolio evaluation pack & customer data sample request

Ben —

Thanks for the clear checklist. We can share a batch but want to confirm a few things before we send anything:

1) Redaction: we will replace names, emails, and account numbers with tokens and keep invoice amounts/dates. Would the [REDACTED_*] format you suggested work for the eval team?
2) Coverage: we’ll include ~120 examples across the three main flow types you listed (invoice short, claim long, edge-case multipart) plus a separate sheet with edge-case annotations.
3) Confidentiality: can you send a short DPA addendum or confirm that the samples will only be used for this evaluation and deleted after 90 days?

We’ll attach the prompt CSV and a first-pass sample set (redacted) by Wednesday 2026-09-22. If that works, please confirm and project owners will route the files.

Elena
Director of Product
BrightFolio
elena.torres@brightfolio.com



From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: BrightFolio Data Team <data-team@brightfolio.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-09-20T10:05:00-07:00
Subject: Fwd: BrightFolio evaluation pack & customer data sample request

Forwarding Elena’s confirmation — attaching a slightly expanded checklist for the eval team. Marissa / Laura, can you review items inlined below and call out which models and benchmark fixtures we should run first?

--- Forwarded message from Elena Torres ---

[attachments: brightfolio_initial_prompts.csv; brightfolio_risk-edgecases.xlsx]

Ben’s checklist (summary for internal ops):
- Run balanced accuracy & hallucination checks on 3 candidate models.
- Produce a short latency-cost tradeoff table for: rw-4-13b-v1, rw-4-7b-q4, and an 2x-distilled variant if available.
- Provide a redaction QC checklist and a recommended tokenization policy for masked fields.

Key considerations: prioritize baseline that meets F1 >= 0.88 with median latency under 300ms on our standard c6 GPU profile. If the 13B hits quality but exceeds cost targets, show a quantized 7B variant run with caching enabled and a sample cost estimate.

Appreciate a quick sanity check — I’d like to share a short plan back to Elena and suggest a time for a follow-up demo on Friday 2026-09-24.

Thanks —
Ben



From: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-09-21T11:03:00-07:00
Subject: RE: BrightFolio evaluation pack & customer data sample request

Ben / Laura —

Thanks for the context and the sample attachments (received). Quick plan and recommendations for the evaluation pass we’ll run as soon as BrightFolio drops the CSV/JSONL:

1) Candidate model slate (first-run):
- rw-4-13b-v1 (balanced): primary quality baseline for extraction + summarization.
- rw-4-7b-q4 (int8-ish quantized): lower-cost, low-latency candidate for production proof-of-concept.
- rw-distil-3-6b (distilled): stress test for throughput-sensitive routing.

2) Benchmark fixtures we’ll produce:
- Quality: entity-extraction F1, summary ROUGE-L, hallucination count per 1k tokens using our eval harness (link below).
- Latency/cost: median P95 latencies and per-1M-token cost estimates with and without prefix caching.
- Regression checks: we’ll run the new prompt set against a control corpus we keep for BrightFolio to flag any quality regressions vs. the control baseline.

3) Redaction & edge-case guidance to include in deliverable:
- Replace direct identifiers with tokens: [REDACTED_NAME], [REDACTED_ACCOUNT], [REDACTED_EMAIL]. Keep numeric fields (amounts) but add a masked_amount field if you want reversible mapping.
- For dates, prefer YYYY-MM-DD. If relative timing matters ("3 days ago"), add an explicit relative_date column.
- Edge-case examples we care about: multi-invoice threads, embedded quoted PDFs, OCR noise lines ("lIke this"), and multilingual customer comments (Spanish snippets). Include them as separate rows with an edgecase_type column.
- PII QC: we’ll run a simple token-detection pass and return a redaction report showing any potential leakage.

4) Deliverables and timeline:
- Day 0 (files received): sanity check + schema validation.
- Day 1–2: run the three-model slate on the provided sample (up to 200 records).
- Day 3: deliver short playbook (PDF) with model recommendations, a benchmarking table, redaction QC results, and recommended rollout config (caching + quantization + recommended SLOs).

5) Quick ask for BrightFolio when they send samples:
- Add a column labeled "safety_flags" if you already mark high-risk items.
- If you have any existing golden-labels for entity spans, include them as span offsets and plain-text labels.

Links & references we’ll attach in the playbook:
- Benchmark report (sample): https://redwood-inference.s3.amazonaws.com/benchmarks/brightfolio/2026-09-20-results.html
- Eval harness README: https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1AexamplePromptSet

If this plan looks good I’ll queue the runs and we can preview the playbook before sending to Elena. Also recommend we propose a 30-minute follow-up slot on Friday 9/24 for a walkthrough — Ben, can you propose a time?

—Marissa
Senior Solutions Engineer | Redwood Inference
marissa.cole@redwood.com

title: Service blend & cost qualification — next steps
preview:
From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Elliot Rogers <elliot.rogers@acme-corp.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 20 Sep 2026 14:05:00 -0700
Subject: Service blend & cost qualification — next steps

Hi Elliot / Claire —

Thanks again for the demo today. Quick recap and the asks we discussed so we can put together a tailored proposal: 

1) Estimated monthly token volume (combined): you said ~90M–140M tokens/month and expect a ramp over Q4. Please confirm a conservative baseline.
2) Target cost envelope: you indicated a target of $800–$1,200 / $1M tokens for customer-facing chat, with embeddings at a separate lower target. Can you confirm whether the $/1M target is an aspirational cap or the procurement target?
3) Model split (initial estimate):
   - Chat: 60% (customer support flows, transactional replies)
   - Embeddings: 30% (semantic search + re-ranking)
   - Rerank/safety-only calls: 10%

Action items for us: Marissa will draft a pricing band and two deployment options (hosted vs dedicated) with estimated unit economics per model. Rishi will include recommended quantization/latency tradeoffs for the chat tier.

Attachments: Pricing profile template + blank SOW (see attached stubs).

Next step: Can you confirm the conservative monthly baseline and whether procurement needs an explicit $/1M commitment for the PO? If OK I’ll get a proposal over by Wed.

Thanks —
Ben
Ben Carter
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.com
+1 415-555-0123

Attachment: Redwood_pricing_profiles.xlsx (spreadsheet stub)
Attachment: Draft_SOW_and_SLA_v1.pdf (pdf stub)

From: Elliot Rogers <elliot.rogers@acme-corp.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Cc: Claire Wu <claire.wu@acme-corp.com>
Date: Mon, 20 Sep 2026 17:12:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Service blend & cost qualification — next steps

Ben —

Thanks for the clear recap. A few clarifications from our side: 

- Baseline: let’s go with 100M tokens/month as a conservative baseline for now (we expect to hit ~125M by Dec).
- $/1M target: $900 per 1M tokens is the procurement benchmark we need to hit for initial approval. That’s for the chat model mix (customer-facing traffic). Embeddings we expect to be ~20–25% of the chat price point.
- Model split: can you treat the embeddings number as mostly vector indexing + occasional full-embedding re-index (so spikes are acceptable). Rerank calls will be small and can be charged differently if needed.

We’d like a two-option proposal: (A) hosted pilot for 3 months with burst capacity, (B) dedicated reserved pool with a 12-month commit and preferred pricing. Procurement will need an estimated monthly invoice figure to justify the PO.

Elliot
Elliot Rogers
Director, Conversational Platforms — Acme Corp
elliot.rogers@acme-corp.com

> (original message)

Ben/Rishi —

I drafted two pricing bands in the attached spreadsheet (hosted pilot vs dedicated commit). Key assumptions called out in the sheet:

- Baseline: 100M tokens/mo chat.
- Chat mix: assume 60/30/10 as Ben suggested.
- Unit economics modeled at: $900 / $1M for chat (target), embeddings at 22% of chat price (~$198 / $1M), rerank at $450 / $1M (smaller but latency-sensitive).

Marissa
Marissa Cole

From: Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwood.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 21 Sep 2026 08:45:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Service blend & cost qualification — next steps

Quick note on the perf/econ tradeoffs —

- If Acme accepts int8 quantization on the chat model and we enable prefix caching for common prompts, we can push the effective chat $/1M to ~$820 (from $900) at 100M/mo. Latency impact: ~5–10% increase on tail p95 for 1–2s interactive flows (still within acceptable UX for support flows).
- Embeddings: 8-bit quantization plus batched indexing lowers cost by ~20% with negligible latency difference for background jobs; for nearline searches we'll use a dedicated pool to keep tail latencies low.

I’ll add a short note in the spreadsheet explaining the technical levers tied to each price line.

Rishi
Rishi Malhotra
Solutions Architect, Redwood Inference

preview:
From: Lucia Romero <lucia@acmefin.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Cc: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 17 Sep 2026 09:12:00 +0000
Subject: URGENT: Two incoming wires — need help allocating to open invoices and VAT guidance

Hi Ben / Laura,

Apologies for the mid-week drop — ACME Finance pushed two separate wires that we need your help allocating. Details below: 

- Wire A (2026-09-15, 14:03 CET): €1,150. Reference: ACME/RED/0915
- Wire B (2026-09-16, 08:47 CET): €820. Reference: ACME/RED/0916

Open invoices in our ledger: 
- INV-1001: €1,200 (goods) + VAT 19%
- INV-1002: €450 (services) + VAT 19%
- INV-1003: €320 (consulting) VAT-exempt (reverse charge expected)

Total outstanding (invoices): €1,970 + VAT adjustments. The two wires total €1,970 exactly, but VAT treatment is mixed and our remittance file only includes wire references — no invoice numbers. Can you confirm how Redwood will apply these receipts and whether VAT should be reclaimed or booked as recoverable?

Attaching the bank remittance notice: ACME_wire_notice_0916.pdf

We'd like allocation and VAT tagging confirmed by Monday EOB (20 Sep) to close our cash application.

Thanks,
Lucia Romero
ACME Finance — Payments Team
accounts@acmefin.com

---


From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Lucia Romero <lucia@acmefin.com>
Cc: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 17 Sep 2026 11:05:00 +0000
Subject: Re: URGENT: Two incoming wires — need help allocating to open invoices and VAT guidance

Hi Lucia — thanks for the heads up and for sending the remittance. I pulled the bank references and sketched a first-pass allocation below based on amounts and typical ordering: 

Proposed allocation (draft):
- Wire A (€1,150) -> INV-1001 (apply €1,150 of €1,200, outstanding €50)
- Wire B (€820)  -> INV-1002 (€450) + remainder €370 -> apply €370 to INV-1003 (which is VAT-exempt)

That maps the totals to €1,970 and leaves INV-1001 with €50 unpaid. However the VAT split needs confirmation before posting: INV-1001/1002 carry 19% VAT, INV-1003 is reverse-charge/exempt. If Redwood posts by applying payments to gross invoice amounts, VAT will be implicitly matched. If we need to book VAT recoverable or create a partial credit note, finance should advise. 

I've attached a quick CSV with the draft allocation: acme_allocation_draft_20260917.csv (headers: invoice, invoice_amount, paid_amount, vat_code). Could Laura confirm the VAT ledger code and whether to create a credit for the €50 residual or hold it on receivable? Also tagging request: include bank trace id ACME/RED/0916 on all postings.

If you prefer, I can schedule a 15-min call to walk through.

Best,
Ben Carter
Account Executive — Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.com
+1 415-555-0192

Attachments: acme_allocation_draft_20260917.csv

My draft allocation (again):
- Wire A €1,150 -> INV-1001 (partial, €50 short)
- Wire B €820  -> INV-1002 (€450) + INV-1003 (€370)

Questions for Laura: 
1) Ledger VAT code: ACME is a German entity — apply VAT-GER-19 to invoices 1001/1002? For INV-1003 should we record reverse-charge code VAT-REV-GER or mark tax-exempt?
2) Residual €50: create a short-payment memo and leave as AR open, or issue a small debit/credit to settle? If ACME requests an automatic offset (they generally prefer credit notes), we should agree wording for the credit note memo.
3) Attachments/traces: I've attached the customer remittance and bank trace we received (bank_trace_0916.pdf) — please ensure the bank reference ACME/RED/0916 migrates to the cash application record.

Can we commit to a firm posting plan by EOD Monday (20 Sep)? If finance confirms VAT reclaim vs credit note, I'll compile the final allocation and send to ACME.

Thanks — Ben

From: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>
To: Lucia Romero <lucia@acmefin.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 21 Sep 2026 15:45:00 +0000
Subject: Re: Post-collection allocation: mapping partial wires to invoices and VAT treatment

Hi Lucia / Ben,

Thanks for the clear notes — finance has reviewed the draft allocation and confirmed the posting approach. Final allocation below (now posted):

- INV-1001 (€1,200 + VAT 19%) — applied €1,150 from Wire A. Outstanding balance: €50. We will leave a short-payment AR open for €50 and include note "partial payment — awaiting customer confirmation". VAT will be matched to the portion paid. VAT ledger code: VAT-GER-19.
- INV-1002 (€450 + VAT 19%) — fully paid from Wire B (€450 portion). VAT ledger code: VAT-GER-19.
- INV-1003 (€320, reverse-charge) — applied remaining €370 from Wire B; because INV-1003 is reverse-charge, we recorded the receipt against principal and will not claim VAT on the item (tax code VAT-REV-GER). The €50 residual across invoices is not subject to VAT reclaim at this time.

Actions performed: 
- Cash application posted 2026-09-21 with bank ref ACME/RED/0916.
- Generated credit note NOT required for now — we will hold the €50 on AR pending ACME confirmation. If ACME confirms that €50 is intended for fee reversal, we will issue a credit note. 
- Uploaded posting proof and allocation CSV to our record. See attachments: ACME_allocation_final_20260921.csv and redwood_allocation_note_20260921.pdf

Lucia, please confirm if ACME would like us to apply the €50 to the next invoice or have Redwood issue a credit note. If we do not hear back by close of business Friday (24 Sep), we'll leave it open per above.

Regards,
Laura Bennett

title: DPA redlines: data transfer acceptance checklist & SCCs/TIA path
preview:
From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Elena Morales <elena.morales@acme-solutions.com>
Cc: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 17 Sep 2026 09:02:00 -0700
Subject: DPA redlines: first pass + checklist request

Hi Elena,

Thanks again for sending the draft DPA. I reviewed the high-level redlines and uploaded a marked PDF (attached) — mostly standard clarifications rather than scope changes. Before we get into line-by-line negotiation, legal at ACME asked for a short transfer acceptance checklist so they can confirm which routes (SCCs vs. Transfer Impact Addendum) you'll accept for various jurisdictions.

Can you confirm the following on your side:
- Are EU Standard Contractual Clauses acceptable as-is for transfers from EU countries?
- Do you require a separate Transfer Impact Assessment (TIA) for Switzerland and any EEA-like jurisdictions?
- Any preferred controls we should call out in the DPA (e.g., KMS, data residency routing, subprocessors notification window)?

If helpful, I've attached our initial redline and a quick SCCs-vs-TIA matrix. If you can send a short checklist back by end of day Friday (your timezone), our legal & procurement teams will prepare a counter that maps to your answers for faster signoff.

Attachments: ACME_DPA_Redline_20260917.pdf, SCCs-vs-TIA-comparison.xlsx

Thanks,
Ben
Ben Carter
Enterprise Sales, Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.com | +1 (415) 555-0192

---


From: Elena Morales <elena.morales@acme-solutions.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Cc: ACME Legal <legal@acme-solutions.com>
Date: Thu, 17 Sep 2026 16:10:00 -0700
Subject: Re: DPA redlines: first pass + checklist request

Ben — thanks for the quick turn.

Short answers to start:
1) EU SCCs: Yes, ACME is generally comfortable with the latest EU SCCs, but our privacy team wants to see any proposed derogations noted explicitly in a short cover memo.
2) Switzerland: We treat Switzerland more carefully — we typically request either an Annex (TIA) or documented legal opinion when the vendor relies on SCCs.
3) Controls: Please confirm key controls: encryption at rest (AES-256), KMS isolation, subprocessor notification within 30 days, and ability to onboard a data processing addendum exhibit with region routing.

I'll circulate your attachments to our privacy counsel and return a checklist document and any follow-up questions by Friday morning CET. We may loop in our global procurement lead depending on the control answers.

Regards,
Elena Morales
Head of Legal Operations, ACME Solutions
elena.morales@acme-solutions.com

Date: Fri, 18 Sep 2026 08:15:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: DPA redlines: first pass + checklist request

Elena — great, thanks for the clarity. Attaching the SCCs-vs-TIA matrix as promised; it calls out a suggested route for each jurisdiction we commonly see in ACME's footprint.

Quick note on controls: Redwood can commit to AES-256 at rest, KMS-managed keys with per-customer key separation on Dedicated/Private deployments, and subprocessor notice within 30 days. For hosted customers we do have a standard subprocessor notification flow that we can shorten to 30 days if ACME requires it — I can flag that with Product Ops.

Suggested next steps:
- If ACME is okay with SCCs for EU transfers, we propose adding a 1-page TIA annex template for Switzerland that lays out the controls and legal basis.
- Laura (cc'd) will confirm our legal position on whether we can commit to the TIA language or if we need to offer an escrow/attestation alternative.

I've attached the SCCs matrix and our redline again. Happy to take a short call tomorrow if that speeds things up.

Attachments: SCCs-vs-TIA-comparison.xlsx, ACME_DPA_Redline_20260917.pdf

Thanks,
Ben

---


From: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Elena Morales <elena.morales@acme-solutions.com>
Cc: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 18 Sep 2026 11:05:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: DPA redlines: first pass + checklist request

Elena / Ben — putting legal context on this:

1) EU transfers: Agree SCCs are the baseline. We should avoid derogations that change controller/processor allocations. If ACME expects any controller-processor swaps we need that flagged up front.

2) Switzerland: Practical options we recommend:
   - Option A (preferred): ACME accepts SCCs + Redwood provides a short TIA annex (technical + organizational measures, subprocessors, routing). This keeps contract simple and is the path we usually take.
   - Option B: If ACME requires a formal legal opinion, we can accept that but it typically adds 3–4 weeks and cost.

3) Exhibit language: we'll propose a TIA annex template that references the SCCs and lists specific mitigations (KMS, region routing, KV caching not enabled for sensitive payloads, logging retention). I attached a short comment file with line-level notes.

Procurement ask: what signoff timeline does ACME expect once we deliver the TIA annex? If they can confirm a 5–7 business day turnaround, we can aim to finalize the DPA within that window.

Attachments: redline-comments-20260918.txt

Karthik Iyer
Senior Counsel, Redwood Inference
karthik.iyer@redwood.com

---


From: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>
To: Elena Morales <elena.morales@acme-solutions.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 20 Sep 2026 17:24:00 -07:00
Subject: Final: DPA path & acceptance checklist

Elena — thanks for your patience while we scoped this internally. Short firm position from Redwood Legal:

- EU transfers: Redwood accepts the EU SCCs as the governing transfer mechanism for ACME's EU-origin data. No material derogations will be proposed; any small clarifications will be captured in the DPA cover memo.
- Switzerland: Redwood will provide a TIA annex (our template) that lists the specific technical and organizational measures; this annex will be contractually attached to the DPA. ACME can use that annex in lieu of commissioning a separate legal opinion.
- Controls: We confirm AES-256 at rest, KMS-managed key separation on Dedicated/Private, and a 30-day subprocessor notification window. We will also include a section on region routing preferences as an exhibit.

Next steps / timeline:
1) Ben/ACME: confirm acceptance of the TIA annex template (we attached it to the HubSpot linked doc).
2) Redwood Legal (Karthik/Laura): will incorporate the accepted checklist items into the DPA and return a final redline within 3 business days after confirmation.
3) Procurement: if ACME confirms a 5 business day review once the annex is delivered, we will route for signature immediately.

If ACME is aligned we can lock in this approach and I will prepare the final documents for countersignature.

Thanks,
Laura Bennett
Head of Contracts, Redwood Inference
laura.bennett@redwood.com

Attachments: (linked) https://docs.redwood.com/s/transfer-playbook

title: Acme migration runway exception request — runtime grace & runbook
preview:
From: Sasha Kim <sasha.kim@acme-ai.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>, Elena Park <elena.park@acme-ai.com>
Date: Tue, 15 Sep 2026 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Request: migration runway + exception for inference-legacy v1 endpoints

Ben —

Thanks for the heads up about the v1 endpoint sunset. We reviewed the migration notes and timeline (team walkthrough attached) and we have a couple of asks before we can commit to the proposed cutover: 

- We run a third-party batching pipeline that will require ~9 weeks to validate against the new endpoint and quantized model; our current internal schedule puts validation starting mid-October.
- We need a short-term exception or extended runway for traffic routing while we complete performance/regression testing. Ideally 60–90 days beyond the announced cutover.
- During the extension window we can agree to a telemetry tag and a weekly sync to share migration progress. We may also need a temporary rate limit increase while we backfill caches.

I attached our migration outline (acme_migration_outline.xlsx) and a redlines doc that procurement will use if we get a firm extension. Can you confirm whether Redwood can grant any formal exception and if so what the conditions/costs are?

Appreciate it — Sasha

--
Sasha Kim
Head of Platform Engineering, Acme AI
sasha.kim@acme-ai.com
+1 (415) 555-0123

Attachment: acme_migration_outline.xlsx (spreadsheet, 128KB)

From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
To: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.ai>, Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>
Date: Tue, 15 Sep 2026 11:05:00 -07:00
Subject: Fwd: Request: migration runway + exception for inference-legacy v1 endpoints

Team — forwarding Acme's ask (below). Key points: they want 60–90 days of runway, will opt into telemetry + weekly sync, and may request a temporary rate limit bump. Procurement will push for written amendment.

Questions I need answered for customer-facing reply:
1) Can Product/Ops support a 60-day exception for a subset of customers onboarding to v2? If so, what instrumentation/flags do we require to limit scope and control cost?
2) What is the recommended commercial approach? Chargeable extended-support, or free grace with traffic caps?
3) Any safety/security review items we need before permitting an extended KV/cache pattern (e.g., data retention, KV leak risk)?

Action items: Karthik — propose technical gating and telemetry requirements. Monica — capture CS comms cadence and what we require from Acme to be eligible. Marissa — flag any rollout constraints for hosted capacity.

I want a short internal proposal by EOD Thu so I can respond with a concrete offer.

Thanks —
Ben
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.ai | +1 (650) 555-0189

Forwarded message attached from Sasha Kim (see original message and attachment).

From: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.ai>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>, Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>
Date: Wed, 16 Sep 2026 09:43:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Fwd: Request: migration runway + exception for inference-legacy v1 endpoints

Ben / team —

Quick proposal and constraints from Product/Ops after looking at Acme's outline:

Proposal (narrow exception lane):
- Offer a 60-day exception window from the date of customer's opt-in. We are comfortable with 60 days as it balances risk and our roadmap.
- Eligibility: only for customers with <5% of total multi-tenant peak token volume on v1, and who sign the amendment accepting an extended-support fee.
- Instrumentation: require an "acme_migration" tag on all requests during the exception plus a flag in their account metadata. Redwood will enable verbose per-request logging for the opt-in window.
- Operational limits: cap burst QPS to a defined throttle (TBD) and require prior notice for any planned burst >2x baseline.

Commercials:
- Recommend a nominal monthly extended-support fee (suggested: $1,500/month) OR usage uplift pricing (0.03 USD per 1k extra tokens attributable to exception traffic), whichever aligns with procurement. The fee helps offset operational support and priority routing.

Risks & mitigations:
- Cache/kv inconsistencies between variants -> require canary testing in our staging and 2 production canary shards configured by Redwood Ops.
- Observability -> we enable additional retention for exception-tagged logs for 90 days.
- Security -> must complete a lightweight security attestation (answers on our standard questionnaire) before opt-in.

Attachments: exception-template.docx (amendment template) — use this for procurement redlines.

If accepted, we should draft a short amendment and a migration checklist (tech runbook + timeline) and include a weekly sync cadence. I can draft the amendment language if you want.

Karthik
Director, Product Operations
karthik.iyer@redwood.ai

--- internal note: Marissa, can you confirm capacity for two production canary shards and whether we can commit to enabling verbose logging for exception-tagged traffic?

From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
To: Sasha Kim <sasha.kim@acme-ai.com>, Elena Park <elena.park@acme-ai.com>
Cc: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.ai>, Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.ai>
Date: Fri, 18 Sep 2026 16:45:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Request: migration runway + exception for inference-legacy v1 endpoints

Sasha / Elena —

Thanks for the clear outline and for being flexible on telemetry and weekly syncs. After internal review, Redwood can offer a limited, conditional exception lane. Summary of the offer: 

- Exception length: 60 days from the customer's opt-in date.
- Eligibility & instrumentation: Acme must add the agreed "acme_migration" tag on all requests and complete the short security attestation checklist. Redwood will enable verbose logging for tagged requests and retain those logs for 90 days.
- Operational limits: burst QPS capped (we'll confirm numeric throttle after capacity check); any anticipated bursts beyond 2x baseline require 48-hour notice.
- Commercials: two options — a flat extended-support fee of $1,500/month during the exception OR per-token uplift (0.03 USD per 1k tokens attributable to exception traffic). Please indicate preferred option.
- Docs & next steps: attached is a stub amendment (exception_amendment_stub.docx) and a link to the shared migration folder. If you accept, sign the amendment via DocuSign and we'll schedule the first weekly sync (Mon 10/05 slot available).

We also require a short migration runbook (high-level checklist + validation steps). Monica will follow up to set the weekly cadence and runbook template. Karthik will circulate the technical gating and canary shard plan for your engineering team to review.

Please confirm acceptance (which commercial option you prefer) and we'll route the amendment for signature.

Best —
Ben Carter
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.ai | +1 (650) 555-0189

Attachments: exception_amendment_stub.docx (word, 14KB)
Links: https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1AcmeMigrationPlan ; https://docusign.com/rooms/ACME-EXT-2026

P.S. Monica / Karthik — I'll need the final throttle number and amendment language by EOD Mon so we can get procurement moving.

title: Pilot billing cadence and temporary net terms request (NeonHealth)
preview:
From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>
To: Liam O'Connor <liam.oconnor@neonhealth.com>
Cc: Diego Fernandez <diego.fernandez@redwood.com>, accounts@neonhealth.com
Date: Tue, 14 Sep 2026 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Pilot billing cadence and temporary net terms request

Hi Liam —

Great to see the pilot kicked off yesterday. A quick note from finance that will help us get you fully configured in our billing system and avoid any interruptions as usage ramps next month.

Proposal (to keep the pilot low-friction while protecting both sides):
- Temporary Net45 for the pilot period (60 days from first invoice) instead of standard Net30.
- 30% upfront deposit to be invoiced at provisioning (applied to the first invoice).
- Monthly invoices thereafter, itemized by model and token bands.
- Because NeonHealth is VAT-registered in the EU, we need a VAT certificate before we can zero-rate the invoices; otherwise invoices will include VAT and will need to be remitted accordingly.

Attached: redwood-credit-application.pdf to kick off a light credit check; if you can return the VAT certificate (neonhealth-vat-certificate.pdf) we'll flag the account for VAT handling.

If Net45 is a blocker, we can consider milestone invoicing (50% upfront, remainder at milestone) — happy to discuss. Can you confirm what works for you by EOD Thursday?

Thanks,
Ava Chen
Head of Sales Operations
Redwood Inference
ava.chen@redwood.com
+1 415-555-0198

Attachment: redwood-credit-application.pdf (application form, 2 pages)

From: Liam O'Connor <liam.oconnor@neonhealth.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>
Cc: accounts@neonhealth.com
Date: Wed, 15 Sep 2026 11:03:00 +0100
Subject: Re: Pilot billing cadence and temporary net terms request

Hi Ava —

Thanks — congrats on a smooth kickoff. We prefer Net30 as standard, but I understand the need to reduce exposure. Two options from our side:
1) Net30 with a capped credit holdback (we hold 20% until 30 days after month-end)
2) Net45 only if we get usage credits against SLA breaches and we receive the VAT certificate (we will send that over today).

We can do 30% upfront if it's required; finance will need formal confirmation of the deposit invoice and GL code to process.

I'll have accounts@neonhealth.com send the VAT certificate this afternoon. If you can confirm the invoice reference you will use for the deposit, we can route payment quickly.

—Liam
Liam O'Connor
Director of Platform Engineering
NeonHealth
liam.oconnor@neonhealth.com

Date: Thu, 16 Sep 2026 08:21:00 -0700
Subject: RE: Internal: NeonHealth temporary terms?

Ava / Marissa —

Quick internal note after reviewing NeonHealth's usage profile and risk:
- They are EU-based; VAT certificate will be key to correct tax treatment. If they supply it, we can avoid charging VAT (reverse charge).
- Pilot projected spend ~ $18k over 60 days; exposure with Net45 + 30% deposit is acceptable if we mark as "pilot" and limit credit to $25k.
- Suggest we approve Net45 for a 90-day temporary window, subject to: VAT certificate received, deposit paid within 7 days of provisioning, and credit application returned (we can run a soft check only).

If Marissa signs off, I'll update the billing profile and set the credit cap.

Diego
Revenue Operations
Redwood Inference

To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>, Diego Fernandez <diego.fernandez@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 17 Sep 2026 09:45:00 -0700
Subject: RE: Internal: NeonHealth temporary terms?

OK — support temporary terms with conditions below.
Conditions we require from finance/legal before changing terms:
1) Deposit invoice (30%) paid and reconciled to GL 4101.
2) VAT certificate uploaded to the customer tax record (we'll keep a copy in the contract folder).
3) Temp Net45 approved for 90 days, after which account reverts to Net30 unless a commercial amendment is signed.
4) Credit limit: $25,000 hard cap; automated usage throttling above $27,500.
5) Add SLA credit language to the pilot MOU: if availability < 99.5% in a month, we issue a credit applied to the following invoice.

Diego — please run the soft credit check and flag any redlines. Ava — if Liam sends the VAT cert, I'll prepare the deposit invoice and sign the MOU for this pilot run.

Marissa Cole

Hi Liam —

Great to see the pilot kicked off yesterday. A quick note from finance that will help us get you fully configured in our billing system and avoid any interruptions as usage ramps next month.

Proposal (to keep the pilot low-friction while protecting both sides):
- Temporary Net45 for the pilot period (60 days from first invoice) instead of standard Net30.
- 30% upfront deposit to be invoiced at provisioning (applied to the first invoice).
- Monthly invoices thereafter, itemized by model and token bands.
- Because NeonHealth is VAT-registered in the EU, we need a VAT certificate before we can zero-rate the invoices; otherwise invoices will include VAT and will need to be remitted accordingly.

Attached: redwood-credit-application.pdf to kick off a light credit check; if you can return the VAT certificate (neonhealth-vat-certificate.pdf) we'll flag the account for VAT handling.

title: FastRetail - Trial success plan & week 1 commitments
preview:
From: Alex Martinez <alex@redwood.ai>
To: Sabrina Cole <sabrina@fastretail.com>, Tom Nguyen <tnguyen@fastretail.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.ai>, Ben Carter <ben@redwood.ai>
Date: Fri, 11 Sep 2026 16:12:00 -0700
Subject: Post-call recap + proposed week 1 commitments (FastRetail trial)

Hi Sabrina / Tom — thanks again for a focused conversation today. Quick recap and a proposed set of commitments to get the 2‑week trial running with clear success signals. Please flag anything off.

Summary of what we heard
- Goal: Validate Redwood inference for product catalog enrichment (title normalization + attribute extraction) on 500k SKUs by end of trial.
- Success criteria (customer-proposed): precision >= 92% on title normalization; extraction recall >= 88% on core attributes; end-to-end latency per SKU (batch) <= 120ms median.
- Data: you’ll provide a 50k sample and a labeled subset (~5k) for evaluation.

Proposed week 1 commitments (kickoff -> prime the trial):
1) Data handoff & access (owner: FastRetail / Tom) — share S3 link and example labeling schema by Mon 9/14 EOD.
2) Infra checklist (owner: Ben) — confirm VPC peering + firewall rules; Ben will share the network_requirements_summary by Mon.
3) Success criteria sheet (owner: Alex) — we attached a template; please confirm metrics and thresholds by Tue 9/15.
4) Runbook + test harness (owner: Redwood / Marissa) — initial runbook and scripts attached (FR_POC_runbook_v1.pdf).
5) Weekly cadence (owner: Alex) — 30m sync Thursdays 10:00am PT; weekly status doc updated via Drive.

Risks & mitigations
- Data sensitivity: if any PII exists we’ll enable token filtering and use private deployment wiring for the trial dataset.
- Edge schema mappings: Ben will allocate time to resolve mapping gaps during the first 48 hours after ingest.

Next steps (actionable)
- Tom: S3 share + IAM role for redwood-poc@redwood.ai by Mon 9/14.
- Sabrina: confirm acceptance thresholds on the attached spreadsheet by Tue 9/15.
- Ben: post network checklist by Mon 9/14.
- Marissa: schedule initial test run for Wed 9/16.

Attachments: FastRetail_success_criteria_sheet.xlsx (xls), FR_POC_runbook_v1.pdf (pdf), network_requirements_summary.txt (txt)

Thanks — I’ll follow up with a calendar invite for the Thursday sync.

— Alex
Alex Martinez
Account Executive, Redwood Inference


From: Sabrina Cole <sabrina@fastretail.com>
To: Alex Martinez <alex@redwood.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.ai>
Cc: Tom Nguyen <tnguyen@fastretail.com>
Date: Mon, 14 Sep 2026 09:05:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Post-call recap + proposed week 1 commitments (FastRetail trial)

Alex — thanks for the clear recap. Confirming a few points and adding details on our side.

Confirmations
- We agree to the proposed success thresholds with one tweak: for extraction recall we’d like to see >= 90% for the three core attributes (brand, size, color) and >= 85% for secondary attributes.
- We’ll share S3 bucket and an IAM role (redwood-poc-access) by EOD 9/14. Tom is looping in our infra (Jared) to help with the role.

Customer asks / asks to Redwood
- Can Ben share an explicit list of IPs and port ranges for our security team? We have a short procurement window and they need transport details.
- We’d like the initial model variant to be the 'redwood-medium-v2' family if possible — we’ll want a second run with quantized weights but later in week 2.

I uploaded a small labeled set (~5,200 rows) to the shared Drive folder and updated the success criteria sheet with our preferred thresholds. Link: https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1a2B3cFR-FastRetail-POC

Thanks, looking forward to Wed’s test run.

Best,
Sabrina
Head of Data Science, FastRetail


From: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.ai>
To: Sabrina Cole <sabrina@fastretail.com>, Tom Nguyen <tnguyen@fastretail.com>
Cc: Alex Martinez <alex@redwood.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.ai>
Date: Mon, 14 Sep 2026 11:42:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Post-call recap + proposed week 1 commitments (FastRetail trial)

Sabrina / Tom — adding the infra checklist details you’ll need for security sign‑off. See inline.

Network & access (summary)
- Source IPs for outbound connections from Redwood: 34.210.11.45; 34.210.11.46.
- Ports: 443 outbound (TLS) for API calls; 22 inbound only for scheduled host-level debug sessions (by exception, via pre-approved Bastion).
- S3 access: we’ll use a role-based cross-account IAM role (redwood-poc-access). Please add the role ARN: arn:aws:iam::888888888:role/redwood-poc-access
- VPC peering note: we’ll initiate peering request once FastRetail shares VPC ID; latency targets for cross-VPC calls are <20ms expected.

Small ask: if you can add one synthetic row that exercises an extreme title (very long, special characters) that helps validate our tokenization edge cases.

I also attached network_requirements_summary.txt to the thread. Will stand by for the IAM role and the Drive link contains the labeled set — we’ll pull and validate tonight.

— Ben
Senior Solutions Engineer, Redwood Inference


From: Alex Martinez <alex@redwood.ai>
To: Sabrina Cole <sabrina@fastretail.com>, Tom Nguyen <tnguyen@fastretail.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.ai>
Date: Wed, 16 Sep 2026 08:20:00 -07:00
Subject: Runbook & kickoff update — initial test run scheduled for today 14:00 PT

Sabrina / Tom — quick update before the test run at 14:00 PT. We pulled the labeled set from Drive and ran a dry validation against the harness — no fatal schema errors. Two small items:

1) Tokenization edge case: Ben found 18 rows that truncate on our current tokenizer; Ben will add a small preprocessing step in the harness to keep gold alignment intact.
2) IAM role: confirmed and validated. Thanks Tom + Jared.

Attached: FR_POC_runbook_v1.pdf includes the exact commands we’ll execute in the 14:00 run and the metrics the harness will emit. After the run we’ll post a results doc to the shared Drive and summarize the acceptance metrics in the sheet.

See you on the call.

Hi Sabrina / Tom — thanks again for a focused conversation today. Quick recap and a proposed set of commitments to get the 2‑week trial running with clear success signals. Please flag anything off.

Summary of what we heard
- Goal: Validate Redwood inference for product catalog enrichment (title normalization + attribute extraction) on 500k SKUs by end of trial.
- Success criteria (customer-proposed): precision >= 92% on title normalization; extraction recall >= 88% on core attributes; end-to-end latency per SKU (batch) <= 120ms median.
- Data: you’ll provide a 50k sample and a labeled subset (~5k) for evaluation.

Proposed week 1 commitments (kickoff -> prime the trial):
1) Data handoff & access (owner: FastRetail / Tom) — share S3 link and example labeling schema by Mon 9/14 EOD.
2) Infra checklist (owner: Ben) — confirm VPC peering + firewall rules; Ben will share the network_requirements_summary by Mon.

4) Runbook + test harness (owner: Redwood / Marissa) — initial runbook and scripts attached (FR_POC_runbook_v1.pdf).
5) Weekly cadence (owner: Alex) — 30m sync Thursdays 10:00am PT; weekly status doc updated via Drive.

Risks & mitigations
- Data sensitivity: if any PII exists we’ll enable token filtering and use private deployment wiring for the trial dataset.
- Edge schema mappings: Ben will allocate time to resolve mapping gaps during the first 48 hours after ingest.

Next steps (actionable)
- Tom: S3 share + IAM role for redwood-poc@redwood.ai by Mon 9/14.
- Sabrina: confirm acceptance thresholds on the attached spreadsheet by Tue 9/15.
- Ben: post network checklist by Mon 9/14.

title: PulseNet SIG/CAIQ follow-up — targeted retention carveouts for ephemeral inference traces
preview:
From: Alyssa Markham <alyssa@pulsenetlabs.com>
To: Grace O'Connor <grace@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Jared Cole <jared.cole@pulsenetlabs.com>
Date: Mon, 14 Sep 2026 15:02:00 +0000
Subject: SIG/CAIQ follow-up: ephemeral inference traces and tenant carveouts

Hi Grace,

Thanks again for the demo last week. The PulseNet security team asked me to follow up with a couple of targeted SIG/CAIQ questions focused on data retention for ephemeral inference artifacts. We are evaluating Redwood for a pilot and need clarity for our SOC 2 auditor.

High-level asks:
- For hosted Redwood API: what is your default retention window for transient artifacts (debug logs, request/response traces, KV cache snapshots, and prompt history)?
- For Dedicated and Private deployments: can retention be configured per-tenant, and what minimum/maximum windows do you support?
- For any carveouts (forensics/legal hold): do you have a documented proof-of-delete and signed attestation process?

I attached our SIG/CAIQ worksheet with the specific control rows we need filled (pulsenet_sig_questions_20260914.xlsx). Would appreciate a response by EOW (Friday 18 Sep) if possible — our auditor wants a documented answer before the end of the month.

Also, if you can surface any example customer language for a targeted retention carveout in the T&Cs / SOW that would be very helpful.

Thanks — happy to hop on a short 20-min call if easier.

— Alyssa
Alyssa Markham
Security Program Manager | PulseNet Labs
alyssa@pulsenetlabs.com


From: Grace O'Connor <grace@redwoodinference.com>
To: Alyssa Markham <alyssa@pulsenetlabs.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben@redwoodinference.com>, Kimberly Park <kimberly@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Wed, 16 Sep 2026 09:18:00 +0000
Subject: Re: SIG/CAIQ follow-up: ephemeral inference traces and tenant carveouts

Hi Alyssa —

Appreciate the clear asks and the worksheet. Looping in Ben (SE) for the technical retention details and Kimberly for SOC 2 / CAIQ mapping. We've started filling the SIG rows and attached a working draft (pulsenet_sig_filled_20260916.xlsx).

Quick answers up front so your auditor has something to work with immediately:
- Hosted API defaults: request/response traces and debug logs — retained for 14 days; aggregated telemetry and operational logs — retained 90 days by default (configurable for enterprise customers).
- KV cache / runtime artifacts: ephemeral cache entries are retained for 7 days by default in hosted, longer only if customer enables dedicated tracing or debugging mode.
- Dedicated & Private: retention is configurable per deployment; typical windows we support are 7/14/30/90 days; Private (on-prem/VPC) customers can set retention to meet regulatory needs and we provide the controls and documentation to demonstrate configuration.
- Forensics / legal hold: we have a documented preservation flow and proof-of-delete procedure; Kimberly can share the SOC 2 control mapping and the CAIQ references.

Ben — can you add a short technical summary and a suggested SOW clause we can propose to PulseNet? Kimberly — if you can attach the keystore/architecture diagram and a one-page attestation template that would close this out.

Alyssa — would you prefer the answers inline in the SIG sheet or a separate explainer doc? We can do both — I'll prioritize the SIG sheet then follow up with a short runbook and diagram.

Thanks,
Grace
Grace O'Connor
Account Executive | Redwood Inference


From: Ben Carter <ben@redwoodinference.com>
To: Grace O'Connor <grace@redwoodinference.com>, Alyssa Markham <alyssa@pulsenetlabs.com>
Cc: Kimberly Park <kimberly@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Thu, 17 Sep 2026 14:07:00 +0000
Subject: Re: SIG/CAIQ follow-up: ephemeral inference traces and tenant carveouts

Hi all — adding the technical specifics and a suggested SOW snippet below. Quoting Alyssa's original points for context.

Excerpted answers (technical):
- Hosted (Redwood API):
  * Request/response traces & debug logs: default retention 14 days; can be increased for enterprise customers up to 90 days with a retention flag in the account settings.
  * KV cache snapshots (runtime): default retention 7 days; these are stored encrypted at rest and rotated per our key rotation policy.
  * Prompt history: we persist request IDs and minimal metadata for 30 days for tracing; full prompt payloads are purged per the trace retention window unless the customer enables longer retention for debugging.
- Dedicated:
  * Retention configurable per GPU pool/tenant. Options: 7 / 14 / 30 / 90 days. Policy changes are applied at the routing layer and logged to the audit trail.
- Private (VPC / on-prem):
  * Customer controls retention via local config; Redwood provides the configuration templates and a verification checklist for SOC 2 evidence.

Compensating controls & proof-of-delete:
- We provide a proof-of-delete runbook describing: identification of objects, deletion job, key-rotation proof, and a signed attestation from the operations team after the job completes. For hosted customers we also provide a deletion audit record (log entry id, timestamp, operator id).
- Keystore architecture: keys are stored in a customer-scoped KMS (customer-managed in Private, customer-key option in Dedicated/Hosted). Attached is a high-level diagram (keystore-architecture-20260916.pdf).

Suggested SOW language (short):
"Redwood will retain ephemeral inference traces (request/response traces, debug logs) for an agreed retention window (default 14 days). PulseNet may request targeted preservation for specific tenant IDs for up to 180 days for investigation; Redwood will provide a signed preservation order and, on completion of the hold, a signed deletion attestation and audit log export."

Deliverables we can share by EOW:
- Completed SIG rows (attached)
- Keystore architecture diagram (attached)
- One-page proof-of-delete attestation template (I will circulate after Kimberly's review)

If you want, we can redline the SOW clause into your procurement template and provide a short appendix that maps to CAIQ control IDs.

—Ben
Ben Carter
Senior Solutions Engineer | Redwood Inference


From: Alyssa Markham <alyssa@pulsenetlabs.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben@redwoodinference.com>, Grace O'Connor <grace@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Kim Security <security@pulsenetlabs.com>, Jared Cole <jared.cole@pulsenetlabs.com>
Date: Fri, 18 Sep 2026 10:23:00 +0000
Subject: Re: SIG/CAIQ follow-up: ephemeral inference traces and tenant carveouts

Thanks Grace / Ben — this is exactly the level of detail we need. Two quick follow-ups from our auditor:
1) Can you provide the proof-of-delete runbook and the signed attestation template (we need both for SOC 2 testing)?
2) We need a short flow diagram showing the deletion pipeline (identify->delete->verify->attest) plus an example audit export (sanitized) showing the delete record for a tenant.

If you can drop the runbook and a sanitized sample audit export into the Drive link you provided (or send via SFTP if preferred), we can close the SIG rows and move to procurement. I've attached our internal request form for evidence collection (pulsenet_request_form_20260918.pdf) — please reference the evidence IDs when you upload.

If it helps, we're happy to accept a redacted sample delete audit record rather than live tenant data.

I'll aim to have our auditor review the materials next week — thanks for the quick turnaround.

—Alyssa
Alyssa Markham
Security Program Manager | PulseNet Labs
