title: Sprint-to-signature roadmap & sequencing for MSA/DPA/order form - NovaHealth
preview:
From: Allan Garcia <allan.garcia@novahealth.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Cc: Samantha Lee <samantha.lee@novahealth.com>, procurement@novahealth.com
Date: Thu, 10 Sep 2026 08:12:00 -0700
Subject: Aligning on sprint to signature — MSA / DPA / Order form dependencies

Ben —

Thanks again for the product walkthrough yesterday. Leadership liked the latency/cost benchmarks and we want to move toward an expedited contract path if Redwood can meet our security evidence windows. I pulled our procurement and legal teams into the thread to surface the sequence we need to close in a two-week sprint.

Top-line asks from NovaHealth:
- A mutual action plan (MAP) that calls out the evidence/artifact owner and due dates (SOC2, data retention, KMS integration notes)
- A minimal order form we can sign to enable a pilot while DPA negotiations continue
- Clarity on whether Redwood will accept our standard DPA vs. Redwood’s redlines (we'd prefer minimal redlines)

Can you send a proposed sequence (who owns each artifact, recommended parallelization, and sign order) and confirm people on your side who can own evidence collection? Our goal: PO + pilot order form in 10 business days; full MSA/DPA sign within 21.

Allan
Procurement Lead — NovaHealth
allan.garcia@novahealth.com

[attachment: procurement_requirements_nova_20260908.pdf stub]

From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Allan Garcia <allan.garcia@novahealth.com>
Cc: Samantha Lee <samantha.lee@novahealth.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Priyom Das <priyom.das@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 10 Sep 2026 10:05:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Aligning on sprint to signature — MSA / DPA / Order form dependencies

Allan —

Appreciate the clarity and the timeline. I put together a first-pass sprint-to-signature skeleton below and attached a compact MAP workbook with lanes for Legal, Procurement, Product, and Engineering. High level proposal: get a pilot order form executed first (enables pilot provisioning) while legal works DPA redlines in parallel with a target of final MSA+DPA signature.

Proposed sequence (short):
1) Procurement OK on pilot order form (Procurement sign) — attach minimal SOW + pilot payment terms (7 days).
2) Redwood provides SOC2 evidence and KMS/HSM integration notes (Engineering + Security) — target 3 business days after order form signed.
3) Legal exchange DPA redlines; Redwood suggests two-tier approach: (a) accept a narrow pilot-scope DPA (no data residency changes), (b) continue negotiation on enterprise DPA in parallel. Priyom (legal) will lead on our side.
4) Order form -> pilot provisioning -> usage data to validate punchlist items -> final MSA/DPA signature.

Owners (Redwood):
- Evidence collection & security artifacts: Laura Bennett (Security Ops)
- DPA & legal negotiation: Priyom Das (Legal)
- Procurement contact / order form edits: Me (Ben Carter) and Marissa Cole (Sales Manager)

I attached the MAP workbook with suggested dates (two-week sprint) and a stripped-down order form sample. If NovaHealth prefers, we can also provide a one-page summary of what would be in the pilot order form.

Next steps I propose:
- Can NovaHealth confirm the single procurement approver and whether PO issuance is required before pilot provisioning?
- If NovaHealth wants a redline back to your DPA, send the DPA by EOD tomorrow and Priyom will review within 48 hours.

I’ll also loop in Marissa to confirm internal SLAs for producing SOC2 artifacts.

Thanks —
Ben Carter
Enterprise Account Executive | Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.com | +1 415-555-0198

[attachment: redwood_map_draft.xlsx stub]
[attachment: draft_orderform_v1.pdf stub]

From: Priyom Das <priyom.das@redwood.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>
Cc: Allan Garcia <allan.garcia@novahealth.com>, Samantha Lee <samantha.lee@novahealth.com>
Date: Mon, 14 Sep 2026 09:20:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Aligning on sprint to signature — MSA / DPA / Order form dependencies

Team —

Quick read from legal: we can support the two-track approach Ben proposed. Tactical notes:
- For the pilot order form: limit data processing scope to test data and a defined pilot environment; include a 30-day automatic termination if pilot doesn't convert — this keeps commercial exposure small.
- DPA: if NovaHealth sends their standard DPA, we will redline to remove open-ended audit clauses and limit subprocessors. We should try to reach agreement on security annex and incident notification timeline within the 10-day window.
- Evidence: Laura, please confirm whether the SOC2 pack includes the latest scoping statement and auditor report (we should mark any missing artifacts in the MAP).

I’ll prepare a DPA redline template and a one-page compromise summary to speed decisioning. Ben, can you confirm whether NovaHealth has a preferred DocuSign flow (single-signer PO vs. multi-signer legal + procurement)?

Priyom Das
Legal Counsel | Redwood Inference
priom.das@redwood.com

Allan / Samantha —

Thanks for the quick sync earlier today. Per our call I’ve attached an updated MAP (tab: Week1-Week3) with explicit due dates and owners. Summary of agreed cadence:
- Day 0 (pilot order form): NovaHealth procurement to approve pilot order form and issue PO or sign via DocuSign (Allan).
- Day 1–3 (evidence handover): Redwood (Laura) to provide SOC2 pack, KMS/HSM integration notes, and a list of subprocessors.

title: Urgent: SentryCore production impact after modelpack v3.2 (p99 latency, errors, throughput)
preview:
From: Paul Nguyen <paul.nguyen@sentrycore.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
Cc: SentryCore DevOps <devops@sentrycore.com>, Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.ai>
Date: Wed, 15 Sep 2026 10:34:00 -0700
Subject: Urgent: p99 latency spike & failed requests after v3.2 roll

Ben / Monica —

We just observed a significant degradation in SentryCore's production inference after the v3.2 rollout this morning (we switched to modelpack v3.2 + quant profile Q8 at 08:00 PT). High-level numbers: p99 latency jumped from ~220ms to 1.2s, 5xxs rose from 0.2% to 1.8%, and sustained throughput dropped ~25% on our search workload. The incident started ~08:20 and has been ongoing; we're seeing some requests time out and a noticeable uptick in noisy hallucinations in the post-process checks.

This is customer-critical (payments fraud detection) and we are in an SLA window — our ops team has asked to request an immediate rollback if Redwood recommends it. We can provide logs and spans. We need an exec-level point of contact; Elena (VP Ops) is copied on this thread and will escalate internally if we don't get a mitigation plan within the hour.

Attachments: sentry_logs_09152026.zip (sample request traces + failure payloads)

Can you confirm receipt and next steps?

Thanks,
Paul
Paul Nguyen
Head of Engineering, SentryCore
paul.nguyen@sentrycore.com

On Wed, Sep 15, 2026 at 09:15 AM Paul Nguyen <paul.nguyen@sentrycore.com> wrote:
> Additional note: we rolled Q8 to save cost on the tail, but the latency degradation looks correlated with longer sequences and prefix reuse.


From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
To: Paul Nguyen <paul.nguyen@sentrycore.com>
Cc: Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>, Irene Choi <irene.choi@redwood.ai>
Date: Wed, 15 Sep 2026 11:05:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Urgent: p99 latency spike & failed requests after v3.2 roll

Paul —

Confirmed, thank you for the quick heads-up and for the logs. We're treating this as P1. Immediate plan: 1) I'll kick off an emergency rollback to modelpack v3.1 for your production route (if you approve), 2) Marissa + infra will triage the quant profile behavior, 3) Monica will handle customer comms and keep Elena updated, and 4) I'll loop in Rishi for any exec-level credits/SLA decisions.

Can you confirm you want the rollback now? If yes we'll aim for a safe rollback with a 10-minute maintenance window and a short validation period.

Attachment: sentry_logs_09152026.zip (received)

Ben Carter
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.ai
+1 415-555-0193


From: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>, Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.ai>, Irene Choi <irene.choi@redwood.ai>
Cc: Paul Nguyen <paul.nguyen@sentrycore.com>
Date: Wed, 15 Sep 2026 13:40:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Urgent: p99 latency spike & failed requests after v3.2 roll

Ben / team —

Initial findings from a quick look at the traces Paul attached: the Q8 quant profile used in v3.2 reduces memory for KV cache per worker which is causing more frequent KV cache misses for longer sequences on SentryCore's workload. When the worker falls back it enters a slower codepath and we observe backpressure that manifests as higher p99 and sporadic 5xxs. We're also seeing increased lock contention in the runtime scheduler for hot shards — that explains the throughput drop.

Short-term mitigation options (pick 1):
- Immediate rollback to v3.1 (recommended) — known-good config, restores previous cache sizing.
- Soft throttle: temporarily limit peak QPS / route to a higher-capacity pool (works if you can tolerate some throughput re-routing).
- Patch Q8 profile: increase KV cache allocation for this workload and push a targeted modelpack (riskier / untested under production load).

I'll stand by to execute the rollback now if we get the go-ahead. Post-rollback action items: full root-cause write-up, reproducer + perf regression test, and a conservative canary plan for reintroducing Q8 with targeted KV adjustments.

Attachment: investigation_notes_0915.md (short doc with trace snippets and suggested config deltas)

—Marissa

Subject: SentryCore incident summary & exec brief — rollback complete, follow-ups and credits

Team / Rishi —

Summary (tl;dr): we executed an emergency rollback to modelpack v3.1 on 9/15 at ~11:25 PT for SentryCore's production route. Rollback restored legacy KV cache sizing; p99 returned to ~230ms within 12 minutes and error rate returned to baseline. Root cause: Q8 quant profile in v3.2 reduced per-worker KV cache allocation which increased misses and triggered slower fallback codepaths for longer-sequence queries — exacerbated by hot-shard scheduler contention on their workload.

What we did:
- 9/15 11:05 — Acknowledged and prepared rollback plan.
- 9/15 11:25 — Rolled route to v3.1 (10m window); monitored golden signals.
- 9/15 11:35 — p99 returned to acceptable range; remained stable through evening.
- 9/16 — Engineering validated reproducible perf regression with perf harness; created reproducer.

Open actions (owners + ETA):
- Engineering (Marissa / Irene): finalize root-cause report, perf regression test, and proposed Q8 profile change by 9/19 EOD.
- Product (Irene): update canary gating rules for quant profile changes by 9/24.
- CS (Monica): finalize customer-facing post-mortem and propose goodwill credits; coordinate with Rishi on credit approval (requested: 2 weeks of discounted reserved capacity) by 9/18.
- Sales (Ben): document impact in deal file and propose optional dedicated capacity to mitigate hot-shard contention.

Customer asks: Elena requested an exec-level brief and a remediation timeline; she also requested a credit discussion. I've asked Rishi to review the proposed credit and approve a path forward.

Attachments: exec-brief-sentrycore-0917.pdf (one-page timeline and impact table)

High-level numbers: p99 latency jumped from ~220ms to 1.2s, 5xxs rose from 0.2% to 1.8%, and sustained throughput dropped ~25% on our search workload. The incident started ~08:20 and has been ongoing; we're seeing some requests time out and a noticeable uptick in noisy hallucinations in the post-process checks.

This is customer-critical (payments fraud detection) and we are in an SLA window — our ops team has asked to request an immediate rollback if Redwood recommends it. We can provide logs and spans. We need an exec-level point of contact; Elena (VP Ops) is copied on this thread and will escalate internally if we don't get a mitigation plan within the hour.

title: Order Form commercial: invoicing trigger language, tax withholding contingency, and grace-window for commit shortfall
preview:
From: Tom Briggs <t.briggs@stellarsys.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: accounts-payable <ap-finance@stellarsys.com>, legal@stellarsys.com
Date: Mon, 14 Sep 2026 16:12:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Draft Order Form — invoicing triggers / tax withholding

Ava — thanks for sending over the draft order form. Quick read-through from procurement/finance here. A few items blocking our internal sign-off:  

1) Invoicing trigger language — Section 2.1 says "invoices issued upon delivery of committed capacity". Can you clarify what constitutes "delivery" for staged commits? We need explicit event triggers so our AP system can auto-issue POs.  

2) Tax withholding / gross-up — Our treasury is in EMEA and they may withhold tax on payments. We typically ask for a clause that requires the vendor to provide tax residency docs and either gross-up or provide evidence of exemption. Can your legal team accept a 30-day evidence window (we can provide certifications within 30 days of first invoice)?  

3) Shortfall/grace window — Stellarsys requests a 60-day grace window for initial quarter commit shortfalls (they prefer not to be invoiced for shortfall until after a cure period). Is Redwood willing to accept a limited forgiveness/cure window for Q1 only?  

We can get legal/redlines over if you want to take this to DocuSign after alignment. Attaching our tax residency template.  

Thanks — Tom
Tom Briggs
Head of Procurement | Stellarsys


From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
To: Tom Briggs <t.briggs@stellarsys.com>
Cc: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwoodinference.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Mon, 14 Sep 2026 18:05:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Draft Order Form — invoicing triggers / tax withholding

Tom — appreciate the quick read and the attachment. High level responses below; attaching a short redline for the Order Form (Stellarsys_OrderForm_v3_redline.pdf) that reflects these asks.  

1) Invoicing trigger: we propose to operationalize "delivery" for staged commits as: (a) the model instance is provisioned and reachable, or (b) when Redwood reports 'Activated' in the activation dashboard, whichever comes first. This gives your AP a deterministic event. Redline adds definition under 2.1.a.  

2) Tax withholding: Redwood can accept a 30-day evidence window to validate Tax Residency certificates. If Stellarsys provides the cert within 30 days of the first invoice, Redwood will issue a credit/adjustment for any withholding applied. We cannot contractually agree to gross-up for third-party withholding at scale, but will commit to cooperation and account-level credit adjustments where evidence is provided. Redline updates Section 6 (Taxes) to reflect this flow.  

3) Grace window for initial quarter: we can accept a one-time 60-day cure window for Q1 shortfalls only, capped at 20% of the quarter target, provided the customer notifies Redwood in writing within 15 days of quarter-end and provides a remediation plan. Added a brief clause in Appendix A.  

Two asks from our side: please confirm the AP billing contact and whether Stellarsys requires Net30 or Net45 payment terms (we typically ship Net30 for self-serve and Net45 for enterprise with PO workflows). Also, please return your legal redline in PDF (tracked) so our counsel can incorporate quickly.

If that all looks OK, I'll loop in Legal (Kimberly) to finalize wording and then send to DocuSign. Proposed times for a 30-min call: Tue 09/15 10:00–11:30 PT or Wed 09/16 13:00–15:00 PT.

Best,

Ava Chen
Senior Account Executive | Redwood Inference
ava.chen@redwoodinference.com
+1 (415) 555-0142

Attachment: Stellarsys_OrderForm_v3_redline.pdf


From: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>, Tom Briggs <t.briggs@stellarsys.com>
Cc: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, 15 Sep 2026 08:22:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Draft Order Form — invoicing triggers / tax withholding

Tom / Ava — small legal note and a proposed redline snippet for exact contract language. Quoting Ava's proposal for clarity:  

> "If Stellarsys provides a valid Tax Residency Certificate within thirty (30) days of the initial invoice, Redwood will issue an account credit or adjustment to reflect any withholding applied by tax authorities. Redwood is not obligated to gross-up amounts withheld by a third party, but will cooperate in good faith to mitigate double taxation."

Proposed clause (to be inserted in Section 6.2):  

"Customer shall deliver to Redwood official tax residency documentation reasonably acceptable to Redwood within thirty (30) days of the first invoice. Upon receipt and verification by Redwood, Redwood will issue an adjustment or credit equal to the tax amount withheld on such invoice. Notwithstanding the foregoing, Redwood shall have no obligation to gross-up amounts withheld by third parties unless expressly agreed in a separate written addendum."

On the invoicing trigger definition: to avoid circularity, replace "Activated" with a technical event name we can surface ("ActivationSucceeded") and include the activation dashboard link in the Order Form as an authoritative source. I added a short definitional paragraph in the redline PDF.  

If Stellarsys needs gross-up language for a specific jurisdiction, we can review on a country-by-country basis, but that will likely require finance/legal approval and may result in a one-time onboarding fee. Please flag jurisdictions if relevant.

I'll be available for a short sync Wed 09/16 10:30 PT if the times Ava proposed are partially workable.

Thanks — Kimberly
Kimberly Park
Senior Counsel, Commercial Contracts | Redwood Inference
kimberly.park@redwoodinference.com


From: Tom Briggs <t.briggs@stellarsys.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: accounts-payable <ap-finance@stellarsys.com>, legal@stellarsys.com, Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Wed, 16 Sep 2026 09:11:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Draft Order Form — invoicing triggers / tax withholding

Kimberly/Ava — thanks, that language is helpful and more concrete. A couple of practical points from finance:  

- AP prefers Net45 for this engagement due to multi-entity consolidation. Can Redwood agree to Net45 if Stellarsys issues a PO referencing the Order Form? If Redwood requires Net30, we need to understand any late-payment penalties or interest mechanics.  

- On the grace window: Stellarsys accepts the capped approach (20% cap) but requests the cure window apply to the first two quarters, not just Q1. Would Redwood extend to Q2 with a review after that?  

- I attached our TaxResidencyCert_Stellarsys.pdf. We'll start requesting tax certs from local entities and push to you within the 30-day window where possible.  

If Redwood can accept Net45 with PO and extend the grace window to Q2 (with the same 20% cap and written notification requirement), we can push to Legal for a tracked redline in the next 24 hours.

Tom

Attachment: TaxResidencyCert_Stellarsys.pdf


From: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwoodinference.com>
To: Tom Briggs <t.briggs@stellarsys.com>, Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>, Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Thu, 17 Sep 2026 11:23:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Draft Order Form — invoicing triggers / tax withholding (ACTION: finalize payment term & DocuSign)

Tom — quick internal confirmation and next steps:  

1) Payment terms: Redwood Finance will accept Net45 for this agreement provided Stellarsys issues a referenced PO that includes the exact Order Form ID. I'll note this as a condition in the final Order Form (Section 4.2) and in the accompanying invoice instructions.  

2) Grace window: Redwood confirms extension to the first two quarters (Q1 + Q2) with the previously stated caps (20% per quarter) and notification/remediation plan requirements. We'll add a sunset review at end of Q2.  

3) Tax docs: thank you for the TaxResidencyCert. Per Kimberly's language, we will verify and issue adjustments where applicable. If gross-up is requested for any jurisdiction, that will be routed to Finance/Legal and may incur an onboarding fee — we'll surface jurisdiction names if that happens.  

4) Action plan: Ava to request Stellarsys legal redline (tracked PDF). Kimberly to ingest final redlines and produce a handoff-ready DocuSign package. I will confirm PO referencing text for your AP team.  

Proposed delivery timeline: final redline received by EOD Thu 09/17 -> Redwood legal finalizes by Fri 09/18 -> DocuSign out Mon 09/21.  

Ava: please confirm AP contact and whether Stellarsys will issue PO reference numbering in the subject line of their PO emails. Once confirmed, I'll update Section 4.2 and send the payable party instructions.  

Thanks all — Karthik
Karthik Iyer
Director, Revenue Operations | Redwood Inference

3) Tax docs: thank you for the TaxResidencyCert. Per Kimberly's language, we will verify and issue adjustments where applicable. If gross-up is requested for any jurisdiction, that will be routed to Finance/Legal and may incur an onboarding fee — we'll surface jurisdiction names if that happens.  

4) Action plan: Ava to request Stellarsys legal redline (tracked PDF). Kimberly to ingest final redlines and produce a handoff-ready DocuSign package. I will confirm PO referencing text for your AP team.  

Proposed delivery timeline: final redline received by EOD Thu 09/17 -> Redwood legal finalizes by Fri 09/18 -> DocuSign out Mon 09/21.  

Ava: please confirm AP contact and whether Stellarsys will issue PO reference numbering in the subject line of their PO emails. Once confirmed, I'll update Section 4.2 and send the payable party instructions.

title: Post-call: technical deep dive / availability accordion proposal
preview:
From: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
To: Anna Schultz <anna@playfin.ai>
Cc: Monica Patel <monica@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-09-15T15:12:00-07:00
Subject: Post-call: technical deep dive / availability accordion proposal

Hi Anna / Playfin team —

Thanks again for the call today — great context on your realtime analytics use case and the latency/cost sensitivity on inference. Quick recap:
- Primary need: scale embeddings + short-form generation for alert summaries (100k reqs/day, median response target ~220ms).
- Data constraints: PII redaction required; KMS-backed keys preferred.
- Success criteria: 95th pct latency under 400ms, predictable cost per 1k requests, and audit logs for retention 6+ months.

Next step: technical deep dive with our Solutions Engineer to walk the infra plan, perf profile, and a short pilot scope. To make scheduling less painful across your EU/US/APAC contributors, I sketched an \"availability accordion\" that expands and compresses weekly windows so the team in each region gets prime hours in rotation. High-level idea: three-week cycle where each week favors a different region, with an overlapping 60-minute bridge window for cross-region stakeholders.

Proposed minimal options (pick one or send alternatives):
- Option A (Week 1: US-friendly): Tue 17:00–18:00 PDT / Wed 00:00–01:00 UTC (works for US lead + EU evening)
- Option B (Week 2: EU-friendly): Wed 15:00–16:00 CEST / Wed 13:00–14:00 UTC (better for EU mornings)
- Option C (Week 3: APAC-friendly): Thu 09:00–10:00 AEST / Thu 23:00–00:00 UTC (APAC mornings)

If you'd rather pick fixed times, send your 2–3 preferred windows and I will build the 3-week accordion and circulate a recurring invite that alternates the primary slot each week. I attached a one-pager mutual-action-plan (stub) and our architecture diagram for context.

Attaching: mutual-action-plan-v1.pdf, redwood-arch-diagram.png

If it helps, I can also copy Karthik (SE) to propose deep-dive agenda items (perf targets, sample payloads, VPC options).

Thanks —
Ben Carter

title: Urgent: sudden 429s after project-tier cutover — OptimaHealth
preview:
From: Joe Allen <joe.allen@optimahealth.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Cc: Priya Shah <priya@ps-consulting.com>
Date: Tue, 14 Sep 2026 08:12:00 -0700
Subject: Production 429 wave — started 07:45 PDT

Ben —

We just started seeing a huge spike of 429s against our primary throughput routes starting ~07:45 PDT. It looks correlated with a scheduled cutover we ran this morning (migrated several projects under a new org-tier mapping). Our customers are seeing timeouts and queued requests. Can you confirm this is on Redwood's side? We need an ETA — this is impacting our caregivers dashboard.

Attached a short trace and the earliest 429 timestamps (CSV).

—Joe
OptimaHealth SRE
joe.allen@optimahealth.com

Attachments: quota-audit-OptimaHealth.csv (csv, 14 KB)

From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Joe Allen <joe.allen@optimahealth.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Naomi Feldman <naomi.feldman@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 14 Sep 2026 08:28:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Production 429 wave — started 07:45 PDT

Joe —

Thanks for the alert and the CSV. We're on it. Initial telemetry shows a concurrent spike in 429 rate and an increase in front-door timeouts for the org id you provided. I'm escalating to the tiering and rate-limit owners now (cc'ing Naomi and Marissa).

Quick asks while we triage:
- Can you confirm whether the cutover was a change to project->org tier mapping or a change to per-project quota?
- If you have any recent infra change IDs (internal cutover ticket) send them over.

I'll follow up once we have the root cause. Apologies — we know this is high impact.

—Ben
Ben Carter
Account Exec, Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.com

Attachments: none

From: Naomi Feldman <naomi.feldman@redwood.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 14 Sep 2026 08:42:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Production 429 wave — started 07:45 PDT

Ben / team —

I pulled the quota and tiering audit for OptimaHealth (see attached). Early hypothesis: during a bulk mapping job that ran at 07:40 the org->project selectors were applied with the wrong precedence, which effectively moved 6 high-throughput projects into a lower quota bucket. That would explain the sudden 429 spike and retry storm.

Evidence:
- quota-audit-OptimaHealth.csv shows per-project limits shaved from 10k req/min to 500 req/min at 07:40:12
- ratechart-20260914.png shows token-generation denials concurrent with their backend retries

Immediate plan:
1) Roll back the mapping job to the previous mapping (can be reverted via mapping-diff patch).
2) Temporarily bump the affected projects to emergency burst capacity while we validate.
3) Ask OptimaHealth to throttle client-side retries (ideally exponential backoff) until we confirm stability.

Karthik, can you stage the rollback and the emergency bump? Marissa, I'll need an exec note for the account (possible credit / SLA exception).

—Naomi
Senior Reliability Engineer, Redwoood Inference
naomi.feldman@redwood.com

Attachments: ratechart-20260914.png, tiering-mapping-diff.patch

From: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>
To: Naomi Feldman <naomi.feldman@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 14 Sep 2026 09:05:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Production 429 wave — started 07:45 PDT

On it —

I'll run the rollback on staging now and then push to prod in a controlled window (targeting +20 minutes). I'll also provision a temporary burst profile (3x) for the six projects. Expecting the rollback + bump to finish by 09:30 PDT.

I'll publish a short post-mortem snippet and a rollback command here so we have an audit trail.

—Karthik
Platform Ops
karthik.iyer@redwood.com

From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Joe Allen <joe.allen@optimahealth.com>
Cc: Priya Shah <priya@ps-consulting.com>, Naomi Feldman <naomi.feldman@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 14 Sep 2026 09:37:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Production 429 wave — started 07:45 PDT

Joe —

We executed a rollback of the mapping job and applied an emergency burst profile to the affected projects at 09:28 PDT. Our telemetry shows 429s dropping back to baseline and request latency improving. Can you confirm whether your dashboards look healthy?

We also recommend applying client-side retry backoff immediately — Priya, if your team can push a 10s jittered backoff, that will reduce load while the system stabilizes.

I'll follow up with an executive summary and a proposal for a credit due to the incident.

—Ben

From: Joe Allen <joe.allen@optimahealth.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Cc: Priya Shah <priya@ps-consulting.com>
Date: Thu, 16 Sep 2026 17:42:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Production 429 wave — started 07:45 PDT

Ben / team —

Confirming the rollback fixed the issue. Dashboards returned to normal within ~15 minutes. Appreciated the quick response — our ops lead will need a short incident note for our compliance review. Could you share the post-mortem and the mapping-diff you applied? Also, we will open a ticket for a follow-up to review quota tiering policies and a request for a one-off service credit against the Sept bill (deal DL-8491).

Thanks —
Joe
SRE, OptimaHealth
joe.allen@optimahealth.com

Hi Ben / Marissa,

Thanks again for the demo yesterday — excited about the performance gains you showed on our conversational SKU. Per our procurement and the MAP discussion, BrightRetail is prepared to provide a small set of representative testcases and a labelled seed corpus to accelerate acceptance testing. Before we hand anything over we need: a brief MAP with milestones, a one-week QA window, and clear usage constraints (no redistribution, no model training, ephemeral sandbox only).

I attached a lightweight CSV \"BR_sample-passport_v1.csv\" with sample identifiers and high-level labels. Legal copied in for any contractual language. Key asks from our side:
- Confirmation we get a time-bound acceptance gate (we propose 2 weeks)
- A clear data handling note describing how Redwood will store, access, and destroy these samples after the pilot
- A rollback/takedown SLA if we discover sensitive data (48 hours)

Can you draft the MAP milestones and confirm a takedown process?

Cheers,
Kate Ellison
Head of AI Partnerships
BrightRetail

---
Attachments: BR_sample-passport_v1.csv (stub)

From: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.ai>
To: Kate Ellison <kate@brightretail.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.ai>, Rafael Mendes <rafael@redwood.ai>, Grace O'Connor <grace@redwood.ai>, BrightRetail Legal <legal@brightretail.com>
Date: Fri, 10 Sep 2026 11:03:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Initial: Reciprocal testcase passport and onboarding

Thanks Kate — and appreciate the CSV. High level: Redwood is happy to proceed with a reciprocal, time-limited exchange. I put together a first-pass MAP and attached a timeline workbook (MAP-timeline-v2.xlsx) that maps deliverables, acceptance criteria, and the rollback window you requested. Summary below: 

1) MAP Milestones (proposed)
- Day 0: Receive BR sample passport + signed MPA addendum
- Day 1–2: Ingest into ephemeral sandbox; hash-only provenance stored in Redwood audit logs
- Day 3–9: BrightRetail runs acceptance tests against pinned model variant (we provide an instrumented eval route)
- Day 10–14: Final acceptance review and sign-off; if accepted, proceed to pilot; if rejected, mutual root-cause and revert plan

2) Data handling and takedown
- Data is stored in an ephemeral, customer-scoped sandbox (separate namespace). Raw files retained for up to 14 days by default, then destroyed.
- If BrightRetail flags PII or a sensitive item, Redwood will pause routing to the sandbox and delete source artifacts within 48 hours; audit entry created.

3) Usage constraints
- Samples may be used for inference-only evaluation inside the sandbox, not for model training or public redistribution.
- Outputs derived from testcases may be used for debugging and acceptance reporting but are subject to the same deletion schedule.

We also propose a short canary run (50k tokens) to validate latency/cost expectations before the full QA window. Legal — I put the MPA addendum draft in the Drive link below; any line items you need highlighted?

I attached the timeline. If that looks good we'll slot Rafael to coordinate onboarding and Grace to own the audit/takedown playbook.

Ben
Senior Solutions Lead
Redwood Inference

Kate — quick note on onboarding mechanics and the \"passport\" fields.

Thanks for the sample CSV; a few asks so ingestion and evaluation are reproducible:
- Please include a stable sample_id and a short provenance note per row (source_dataset, collection_date) so our acceptance report can trace back to originals.
- For any redacted fields, include a checksum or token so we can verify redaction consistency without seeing raw text.

Operationally we'll ingest into a customer-scoped sandbox cluster with isolated KV caches. We will:
- Generate a test-run audit (sha256 of file manifest), stored in the audit log and viewable to BrightRetail via the Console
- Run the instrumented eval route with logging turned on for token counts and model variant used

One more thing: for the \"no-training\" constraint we enforce a technical guard — the bucket is mounted read-only for the runtime that powers the eval route and training pipelines are blocked for that namespace. We'll add the guard language to the MPA addendum.

I'll schedule a 30-min onboarding session with your SRE and ours for Wed. Proposed slots: Wed 9:30 or 14:00 PT. Which works?

Best,
Rafael

1) Retention exception process
- We can support a 30-day forensic hold in exceptional cases but it must be requested in writing via legal@redwood.ai and include the specific sample_ids and justification. Once approved, we place a legal hold and lock deletion for only the listed items; all other data follows the 14-day schedule.

2) Takedown & redaction SLA
- If BrightRetail flags PII, we will: (a) pause access to the sample immediately; (b) run a redaction verification pass; (c) delete source artifacts for that sample within 48 hours; (d) provide a takedown certificate and audit entry to BrightRetail.

Next steps:
- Ben to finalize MAP timeline and push MPA addendum to the shared Drive (Ben, can you confirm you pushed v1.1 with the retention clause?).
